title: I used the ordinary hyaluronic acid…
body: I used the ordinary hyaluronic acid serum and it was lovely no problems at all. I then decided to purchase their niacinemide 10% + zinc 1% and after just 3 days my face and neck was burning quite badly and I had red patches in places. I received a questionnaire from the ordinary asking how I was getting on with this product, as soon as I mentioned this product had burnt my face and neck, their questionnaire suddenly disappeared, gone! This says it all, they don't care and they're only interested in taking our money and looking for 5 🌟 ratings. I won't ever buy from them again and in fact I now purchase my skincare from Holland and Barrett, excellent products and excellent customer care service, I now have beautiful skin and I'm very happy indeed 😊

title: Customer service is terrible
body: Customer service is terrible. Takes forever to get back to you, if at all. They don’t care and will fill the email with emojis to make it feel more fun while they tell you nothing about your order. I made a large order for a birthday gift, for someone who had never tried their products but wanted to based on my recommendations. The order never arrived and the ordinary told me it was because of bad weather. It’s been sunny and warm here for the entirety of winter. I have received other orders from other companies with no issue. The ordinary basically told me to kick rocks. Did not offer to replace my order or find out where it went. Won’t order from them again.

title: I like the product, but the customer service is zero-star.
body: My parcel was marked as “delivered” by Royal Mail on 27 January. By “delivered,” I mean it was left unattended in the ground-floor foyer of my apartment building — no concierge, open to anyone passing by.When I reported this to Customer Service, they claimed to have “investigated” and then told me that because Royal Mail marked it as delivered, it was now my responsibility to chase Royal Mail myself. This is unacceptable.You are the seller and the shipper. I paid for postage. It should not be the customer’s responsibility to resolve a missing parcel caused by the courier you chose. This should be covered under Royal Mail’s lost parcel process, which you should be handling — not passing the burden to the buyer.Very poor customer service and a complete lack of accountability. I regret not reading the Trustpilot reviews before placing my order.

The Ordinary works with UPS which isn’t the best delivery service (maybe even the worst?) and you can only reach the customer care through email (or phone if you want to really up your phone bill). Really love them and most definitely going to keep buying them, but also most definitely through a reseller like Douglas:)

title: Good beauty products - with ugly baggage attached.
body: I'm discontinuing my purchases from The Ordinary (online or in-store) for two key reasons.First, the delivery experience with DHL in Spain has been consistently terrible. Across multiple orders, they've repeatedly claimed my address is "incorrect" and forced me to travel to the nearest city (1-hour round-trip) to collect packages from a DHL point. My address is always supplied 100% accurately – and DHL has successfully delivered dozens of orders from other companies to this same address over the past decade (as have countless other delivery companies). This is clearly a service issue specific to The Ordinary's logistics arrangement.Second – and more importantly to me in light of the world we're now living in – I'm uncomfortable with the brand's ownership structure. The Ordinary is owned by Estée Lauder Group, and until recently, Ronald Lauder (founder's son) served on the board and donated $5 million to Trump's MAGA Inc. super PAC. He's also credited with originally suggesting to Trump the idea of purchasing/invading Greenland. While he retired from the board in January 2025, the family maintains significant influence over the company.Between the delivery nightmares and ethical concerns about the parent company's icky political connections, I'll be taking my business elsewhere.In the meantime, my The Ordinary stuff will be going in the bin – they’re actually good beauty products on the surface, it’s just a shame they come with such ugly baggage attached.

title: Excessive Packaging and Poor Customer Service from The Ordinary
body: The Ordinary’s poor packaging and unresponsive customer service made a simple order unnecessarily stressful, ignoring my key concerns entirely.My ExperienceI ordered three serums and a balm, but they arrived in an oversized 460x400x280 mm box that created excessive waste and disposal hassle. One serum was missing upon receipt (the box had been left in a communal area and arrived tampered with), and while they eventually agreed to replace it, the massive packaging overwhelmed my recycling and added unfair disposal costs on my end.Company ResponseTheir reply focused solely on the missing item, offering no acknowledgement of the broader packaging issues or the frustration it caused. The initial email I sent bounced back, forcing me to retype my full concerns into their restrictive web form that didn’t allow copy-paste, making the process even more tedious and stressful. No meaningful action followed, just a generic feedback note and a choice between refund or replacement, disregarding everything else I raised.Key Issues• Excessive Packaging: Unnecessarily large box for small items shows lack of environmental responsibility.• Customer Service Barriers: Bouncing emails and non-copy-paste forms hinder proper communication.• No Accountability: Empathy was expressed, but zero resolution for my actual concerns or added burdens.A extremely disappointing experience overall. Seek brands with better packaging and accessible support.Update: January 20 2026The Ordinary’s reply admits they “did not receive the initial message,” yet my summary, submitted through your restrictive form on January 14, explicitly stated the excessive packaging overwhelmed my recycling and caused disposal hassle. They ignored it entirely then, and now vaguely promise to “pass feedback” without addressing the environmental waste or my burdens.Follow-up email reply with photos of the tampered box and missing serum bounced initially, forcing retyping due to no copy-paste, PLUS a resend via copy and paste in italics of my original detailed email. Still ignored. Pure frustration. Replacement is coming, but no accountability for core issues. Stop excuses; act on packaging now. Still 1 star.

title: No customer service
body: UPDATE: The Ordinary reached out and after a few discussions, we came to an agreement to resolve the issue. So I'm upping my rate from 1 to 3 to highlight their efforts. ORIGINAL : I like the products. But the customer service is non-existent. The FAQ didn't answer my question. The chat bot stated that no one was available to answer my questions and ended the conversation. I called the number that put me on hold with a message that said they were receiving a lot of calls... and then hung up. So I filled in the form asking if I could modify my order. I received an automated reply that didn't answer my question either. My question was very simple: can I modify my order to add a product I forgot to include? I got a silly automated reply that gave me their return policy, followed by an email saying my order had shipped. I guess it's too late to add anything... This followed a situation a few months back where I was supposed to pick up my order in store. But the order was sent to the wrong city (my browser messed up). The teenage Kim K at the store said I had to get in touch with customer service, and refused to put the order together. When I read the policy online, it stated that orders not picked up would be reimbursed after a few days. Couldn't Kim K say that instead of directing me to a non-existent customer service and leaving me infuriated?

title: Watch for misleading advertising!
body: I was a loyal customer of The Ordinary for a long time, until I placed an order specifically because of their well-advertised gift-with-purchase promotion lasting a whole week. I ordered right on the day the promo started and the gift was simply not included. When I reached out, customer service replied with a disappointing “while supplies last” explanation asking me to watch for future opportunities. It made the promotion feel misleading and the response careless. I would honestly caution others to manage their expectations when it comes to these promotions.

title: Customer service is non-existent
body: I would continue to give this company a zero if it was allowed. I rated them zero last year as well. The customer service is non existent. I was supposed to receive a package Dec. 4th and here it is the 14th and still no package. UPS claims we have had 10 days of "severe weather". I'm quite sure the package is lost and i contacted The Ordinary, on Dec. 5, asking them to contact the courier. Not til Dec 14 did they tell me they have contacted the courier but basically its out of their control. I am amazed how easily they just wash their hands of a problem and think they are delivering "happy customer service". Especially when the customer has no control over the courier that is used. Good customer service would have been contacting the courier Dec. 8, as 6&7 was the weekend, and then sending me a new package with a reliable courier and have the other shipped back to them. I could have at least had it for Christmas. Sending me an email telling me its out of their control, 10 days later and suggesting I look at their FAQ is just offensive. I didn't learn last year, but I will not make this mistake again. I'm done with this company.

title: Issue with Missing Promotional Gift and Order Handling
body: I noticed there was a promotion saying that any purchase made before 14/11 would come with a free serum. However, when my order arrived, the gift was missing. I reported it to the team immediately on the same day I received the package (which was on the 16th).On the 20th, I got a reply saying that if the item was missed in the shipment, I would need to make another purchase in order to receive the free gift. This doesn’t make sense, because I only bought everything before the deadline because of the promotion.Just a reminder to everyone — make sure to double-check your orders. There seem to be quite a lot of mistakes and missing items.

title: I recently ordered the foundation
body: I recently ordered the foundation, (oops I think poss. from Boots), anyhow I am in my late 50's with dryish skin and darkish circles under my eyes (genetics), I really like this product, initially it looks like peanut butter but it goes on well (full coverage). I have tried other products and yes some of them can sting a little but they are acids, it's only momentarily, most beauty treatments at a salon sting a bit too.Most of the negative reviews here seem to be for customer service and items not being delivered, but the products themselves for the money are some of the best on the market.Over a few weeks there has actually been a difference in my skin (for the better).

title: Ok so I just started with The ordinary…
body: Ok so I just started with The ordinary and Squalane cleanser feels like a soft balm that melts to oil and rinses clean without tightness. Niacinamide 10 percent plus zinc 1 percent is a bit tacky and can pill if I rush, but the dropper is precise so I wait a minute before layering multi peptide plus HA serum which sinks in nicely. AHA 30 percent plus BHA 2 percent peeling solution looks scary red iin the glass bottle and tingled lightly without irritation, while granactive retinoid has a gentle milky slip and seems to avoid a big purge so far. Shipping was quick and everything arrived well packaged, there is a learning curve for order and spacing, but I already notice smoother texture and tighter looking pores.

title: The Ordinary has stolen my money and lost my order
body: Do not order from The Ordinary/DECIEM website. They lost my order, provided no valid tracking information, and have still not refunded me. They have simply taken my money and stopped responding.When I contacted their so-called “Happiness Team,” they blamed me for an “insufficient address” even though I entered my full address manually several times and their checkout system kept auto-changing it. I explained this multiple times, but instead of taking responsibility, they gaslit me and insisted it was my fault.UPS confirmed that only the sender (DECIEM) could correct the address, yet they told me to “contact UPS myself.” I did, and UPS made it clear that DECIEM needed to fix it because they have literally locked that option to change address on their end. DECIEM has since gone silent, leaving me without my order or my refund.Their customer service is robotic, dismissive, and unwilling to resolve issues they clearly caused. And this isn’t an isolated case. I can see Trustpilot is full of identical complaints of lost packages, no tracking, no refunds, and the same blame-shifting responses.A reputable company would never keep a customer’s money after failing to deliver the product.Save yourself the stress and frustration. Buy from a verified retailer instead, The Ordinary’s own customer service is not trust-worthy, professional or ethical.One has to wonder at someone who has taken no action even after seeing all these similar complaints pile up. Gross.

Bravo!

title: I still didn't receive my order
body: I placed my order (21511674) on August 11. The next day it was shipped via UPS. 10 days later I still didn't receive it. UPS tracking page stopped updating on August 18th with the message saying the sender's details are incorrect, therefore, package cannot be delivered. It says they need to be provided with the correct address to put the parcel out for delivery. I cannot do it on my end. This option isn't available for recipient. I cannot get hold of UPS customer support. I sent a message to The Ordinary support on August 19th to ask to handle the problem and so far got only automatic response that the message's been delivered and that someone will return to me shortly. I tried calling, the waiting time is too long to wait for someone to answer. The chat option keeps saying "no agents available" even though it's been 3 days that I try to connect with a real person at different times several times per day and within working hours of support team. So writing here is my last resort. Can someone please check what's happening with my package, provide UPS with whatever info they're missing, so I could finally receive my order? It's the first time I buy directly from The Ordinary and, frankly, the experience is not great so far. I do like your products though. I've been impatiently waiting to test new products I bought, specifically, the niacinamide powder. I know it will be taken off market soon. I thought I'd try it and will have time to bulk buy it before it disappears, now I start thinking if I will ever be able to test it at all.. So, please, can someone look into it?

title: Scam with subscription
body: I ordered a bottle of Glycolic acid 7% because it works well with my skin and I love the product. Signed up for the subscription and for the renewal date to be July 12, 2025. To my surprise I saw where they kept on processing the transaction on my bank account three times and also sent emails that my card was not processing the transaction.Sent an email in regards to the issue and they were not able to confirm why or what could have caused such discrepancy. I went ahead and removed my card from my account and will not be doing any subscription with them anymore. These companies are a rip off with these subscriptions.

title: Highly recommended! 💯
body: I ordered 6 different products from The Ordinary after completing the questionnaire to assess what my skin needs.I was recommended a product from a friend but got so much more advice/knowledge from doing the questionnaire so I ended up buying all that I needed.I’m so happy with my purchases. I noticed a visible change almost instantly and 4 days later my skin is glowing ✨ it’s like it had a big refreshing drink after using hydrochloric acid and niacinamide and my hyperpigmentation faded after one use of the glycolic acid. The squalane cleanser, the moisturiser and the spf are all great products too. So I highly recommend.I did have a slight issue with delivery from UPS. I wasn’t home when they attempted delivery so they delivered it to a random shop (not my nearest) so I had to go and collect it from there. Not a problem for me as I drive but could be an issue for others.My only suggestion to ‘The Ordinary’ is that you change your courier company as I see on other reviews that others have had issues with UPS also.I wouldn’t reduce my rating due to the courier because the products themselves are amazing! ✨ I’m very pleased and excited for my ‘glow-up’ because if this is the result in 4 days.. I can only imagine my skin in 6 months time!No more make-up is my goal 😁👌🏾

title: Ignored Cancellation Request
body: Ignored Cancellation Request – Poor Customer SupportI placed an order with DECIEM/The Ordinary on 21st June and submitted a cancellation request within 25 minutes — well within their official 1-hour window and during business hours.I sent multiple requests through their website and even followed up via Messenger, but received no reply at all. Despite this, the order was still shipped and delivered.I’ve now had to return the item at my own cost, which could have been avoided if customer service had simply acknowledged my request.this experience has made me seriously question their customer support and how they handle customer concerns.

title: I cannot fault their products
body: I cannot fault their products. They work and the value for money is amazing. I have used (and continue to order) The squalane cleanser, the Ascorbic acid serum, the retinol, the matrixyl, the HA, the brow serum and the rose hip oil - all AMAZING. The only thing that I didn't rate was the multi-peptide eye - it was expensive and my eyes reacted to it. Otherwise, the products are amazing. It's a shame that they're clearly having major issues with their postage and customer service. I have ordered direct with no issue in the past, but now tend to use ASOS as next day delivery.

title: They will lose your package. Go through a 3rd party
body: Waited over a month for an order that never came. Checked my email and they ended up giving me a refund which takes almost 2 weeks real time instead of re shipping it. I have been using this company for years and honestly the quality when it comes to receiving items in a timely fashion has gone down severely. MY items were apparently "lost in transit". So if you want the ordinary products, go through a 3rd party to get it because you may not ever receive the items you order off the site. It's worth paying a few bucks more to actually get your stuff.

title: Do NOT order from their own website

title: Non-existent Cutomer Service.
body: The ordinary has fantastic high quality products that I personally use and love, however their customer service is appalling and really let's them down.I ordered directly from their website and didn't receive a confirmation email or order number, nothing.I then reached out to their email which took about an hour of searching before I found it on reddit and not their website, I sent an email requesting my order number and was sent back a bot saying that they would reply soon and I could talk to live chat if I wanted.This mysterious live chat does not seem to exist anywhere on this earth, I still have not received an email response.I went to instagram and DM'd them, it took nearly 6 days to get a response by which time I had spent hours trying to find my order and did eventually find it on my own.Again the only customer service they offer appears to be bots.I have finally received the long awaited order number and confirmation after nearly 10 days. I have yet to receive my order though so we'll see if it even gets here.At this stage I do not have much hope left.Maybe a robot will deliver my order, just like all the other interactions I've had with this company.Edit: I have received my products, the box was in good condition and everything was there. I have updated my star rating for this reason.If you have no issues with your order the company is great with wonderful products.If you do then the customer service is practically useless. 3 stars.

title: Impossible to receive package in Hungary
body: It is literally impossible for me to get the products I ordered!!!!I first ordered on 16/November/2024 /(100€ worth of products) and reached out to Deciem as UPS claimed I don’t want the package and sent it back. The reshipment was on 3/december, however I asked the customer service agent if they could help me sort out the problem and they basically informed me there is never any guarantee that I will receive my package. When UPS again claimed that I “refused delivery” without ever contacting me I tried to reach out again but my case was already closed. Why is a problem with no solution in sight marked as solved? And how can a courier claim I refused delivery when the package never even left their warehouse?This is not a personal issue, many of my acquaintances said they don’t order from The Ordinary anymore because it’s impossible for them to get the packages in Hungary.I contacted the customer service numerous times, they are completely unhelpful, unprofessional, take zero responsibility and do not care about the customer. It feels like I’m either talking to the same robot over and over again or 12 year olds instead of adult human beings as they can only answer with the same 2-3 sentences regarding any issues. It’s ridiculous to read “sorry for the issue love :(“ and “You are welcome to replace your order anytime :)” with smileys and hearts when we are adults communicating over a huge problem, which is obviously a 100% the couriers fault!! It is your job and responsibility to contact the courier that you choose to work with and which represents you!And to add, I do not understand how it’s worth it for the company to send two packages back and forth over borders…

title: The Ordinary- you're truly worthless
body: The Ordinary- your lack of concen about your customers makes you worthless.I have been a loyal customer for many years, relying exclusively on their skin care but this year's experience has permanently changed it for me. Deciem is a horrendous, despicable company making the world a worse place.The Ordinaryis the epotomy of arrogance of big brands disrespecting and defecating on a customer without any accountability to the issues they themselves create.After trying to get my order for 3 months-an experience that I wouldn't wish upon my worst enemy, I am now officially moving om from this brand.First delivery- After the mail that the package is dispatched, I got no notification, even though they had my phone number and email that the package was down for pickup. When I checked the status of my order it was already returned to the sender. NO NOTIFICATION!Then there is their customer service which just spits in your face. I contacted their customer care via their online form. Someone got back to me with a proposal that I can get the order refelivered or the refund. The email gave me 48 hours to respond. It was a late evening when I got the email so I didn't respond straight away. Around 10hrs later I got another email THANKING ME FOR RESPONDING (!!) and processing refund. My wish was to actually have a redelivery since I can't purchase these specific items in any shop in my town!I can understand a glitch but blatant lying to my face that I have responded while in fact I took no action is a punch in the face.I wrote to them and got an email that the escalation team will take care of this.The escalation team, after two-weeks wait, actually got back to me offering to send part of the order, telling me that it'll be sent to me shortly. That was a month ago and the last I heard from them. I tried to write to them but they closed the case and don't answer to any more communication via their online form.The thing is that my local store doesn't store the items I have ordered, so my only choice is to get them from Deciem's website.So, to my fellow customers, if you're genuinelly making an effort to buy, waste your time for months just to be totally ignored, would you stay loyal to the brand?If someone from The ordinary is reading these don't bother replying with your standard "We are sorry for the inconvenience" nonsense. It doesn't mean anything to anyone and just showcases your lack of concern, therefore worth.Thank God the skincare industry is highly competitive with loads of brands on the market so I can easly chose anything but this horrendous brand. I just really regret all these years of being loyal to them and supprting them. Never again, thank you Deciem for teaching me that lesson yourself.

title: Very negative experience with customer service on how they handled my inquiry
body: Very negative experience with my first on-line order. Ordered the products and it shows a shipping label was generated 2 days later. After about 10 days, still no show of the products. Checked the order status, still showing shipping label generated but courier does not have the order yet. Reached out to the customer service, was told to check the tracking provided. I told her that I already checked the tracking it shows it has not been handed over to the courier yet, so I am doing that customer service;s job checking the tracking and telling her what is happening. Magically the parcel was picked up after and got delivered 2 days later. Amazing those customer service has not customer service skill and has no common sense at all. She should be re-trained or fired.Jan.14-2025 - I provided the additional informationJan.14,2025 - Thanks for your reply below. Do not blame this on Canada Post strike and carrier's got more parcels than they can handle.If your customer service has done what she was supposed to be doing, she would have checked UPS website for an update and found out that the package was never handed over to the courier since Nov. 27.& magically my message back and forth with your customer service, the package was picked up.Do not call your Customer Happiness Team. If you want to make customer happy, admit the mistake and go forward. Do not blame the strike. Just shows how bad The ordinary customer service team is.

title: I am very surprised reading some of the…
body: I am very surprised reading some of the negative reviews on here. I have used The Ordinary products for years and have ordered directly from DECIEM for years too. I've always been happy with the service. The products are excellent and well priced. Orders are sent quickly via UPS with full tracking.

title: the worst online shopping experience
body: If I could give zero star , I would give just nothing.I am writing to express my deep frustration with my recent attempts to place an order on The Ordinary/Niod website. For the past weeks ,I have repeatedly encountered the same error message: "We're sorry that your order could not be placed. This probably happened due to a high order volume or temporary connection errors......."I reached out to your customer support team via email for assistance, and unfortunately, the experience has been far from satisfactory1. I was advised to contact my card issuer/bank to have transactions on your website unflagged. This suggestion is quite surprising, as I have used this card successfully on numerous other websites for the past five years without any issues. And there’s nothing wrong with my cards, I have tried different cards, I am sure your company just ban customers from buying during the Black Friday sales for example.2. After trying to use another credit/debit card, still can not place an order3. I have attempted every possible solution, including creating a new account, adding new payment methods, and even placing an order as a guest - nothing works.This has been an incredibly time-consuming and disappointing experience. I have never encountered such difficulties with online shopping on any other platform. I strongly urge you to review and update your current system to align with modern standards, as the existing processes are causing significant inconvenience to customers.

Update says that package will be delivered by end of today, no idea what that time is, but tracking is showing since early this morning that it’s on its way but not yet out for delivery. My account at The Ordinary shows it hasn’t been shipped and today’s tracking 7/12 on UPS says delivery details will be provided as soon as possible. So Im confused already. UPS don’t seem to know what they’re doing. When they start getting things wrong like this it doesn’t fill me with much hope. Wish I hadn’t bothered.

title: Disappointing customer Service
body: This review is NOT about the product (which is great and I love). It is about my parcel which got delivered to a UPS Access Point that is not operating anymore and how customer Service handled it. I contacted UPS about the lost parcel and they said the receiver cannot file a claim/complaint. So I asked The Ordinary's customer Service to help me. However, they refuse to contact UPS. It would be a simple complaint with UPS to trigger a search or refund! How hard can it be! This goes on for 2 months now. Very disappointing.

title: Completely Unacceptable Service
body: I am absolutely furious with my recent experience with The Ordinary. My last three orders have been a complete disaster. The previous two orders were delivered to a pickup point instead of my home address. While I was at least provided with tracking information on those occasions, this time, I never received any tracking details at all for my order.Only after I contacted customer service, I found out that my package (order 19610386) is being returned to the warehouse because the UPS courier supposedly couldn’t deliver it. This is beyond frustrating, especially since I’ve been home for the entire month and available to receive the delivery.The complete lack of communication and failure to deliver my order is unacceptable. The entire experience has been extremely disappointing. I will seriously reconsider making any future purchases from this company.

title: Poor communication
body: So, placed an order during their Nov sale, order was confirmed and my credit card billed. Then on Black Friday when their company is closed, I receive an email advising my order has been canceled. No further information or reason given. Of course since they are closed Fri and the entire weekend, I can't get an answer. More information would have been helpful. Maybe not sending cancelation emails when a repeat customer can't reach anyone would be a good business idea. (Order # 19939482).Update, apparently because one of the items on my order was out of stock 4 days later, they cancel the entire order. Not a great business practice since I still would have liked the other product. I was told I'm welcome to place another order. After this experience? Not likely....

title: It works! Covent garden branch - lovely staffs and manager
body: I always recommend Ordinary brand skincare to my patients because I know their products work magic.Does it work? I can wholeheartedly say yes! As a satisfied customer, I always stock my favourite Ordinary skincare products for my daily routine. These products genuinely deliver on their promises and have saved me a significant amount of money compared to expensive brands that fell short of my expectations. I genuinely believe that Ordinary products are dependable, results-oriented, and budget-friendly.If you're overwhelmed about what to choose, I highly recommend visiting the Ordinary store in Covent Garden. The staff there, including the manager, are amiable and supportive. They take the time to understand your unique skin needs and offer thoughtful guidance on which products might be best for you. You’ll leave feeling informed and confident in your choices!

body: The glass bottles break so easily as do the droppers. I was taking a brand new bottle of serum out of the box when it fell out of the other end and shattered all over my floor. title: Pending Commission Payment
body: Dear NISALINK & THE ORDINARY team,I am writing to request an immediate review of the pending commission payment for the brand "The Ordinary." Since March 2024, we have been generating sales for this brand, and while we received commission payments for May 2024 orders, all subsequent commissions from orders dated May 10, 2024, onward remain unapproved and unpaid.The outstanding commission amounts to approximately $4,000, which represents a significant sum for our business. Despite patiently waiting for over six months, the delay has become untenable. We kindly ask Nisalink and The Ordinary to promptly approve and release the pending payment.Should this issue remain unresolved, we will have no choice but to involve our legal team to initiate formal proceedings against both The Ordinary and Nisalink.Info:Account Id: 337653Thank you for your prompt attention to this matter.--Best Regards:Nail Kelvin Affiliate ManagerShoppingcutt ltd UK

title: excellent products!
body: I can’t speak for delivery or ordering through the website as I purchased in store at a Boots in my area; but I am a huge fan of their products!I’ve been eager to try their AHA & BHA peel, as I have incredibly sensitive skin and I fear a ‘true’ chemical peel would be too harsh, but I also had concerns for the alternative product mentioned too. I went ahead and purchased after weeks of research to figure out how to apply and what to do/what not to do and I’m so glad I went with it!Paired with a vitamin C spf and a vitamin E enriched moisturiser with hylauronic acid, I’ve found a skincare regime that works perfectly with my skin!My skin no longer looks red and inflamed, or grey and dull when the redness is managed!I’ve genuinely never experience skin this glowing, soft and clear since pre-puberty!I love the dropper feature too, as it means I can apply half a dropper twice a week, or one full dropper once a week!Will definitely be using as long as possible!

title: I recently started using The Ordinary…
body: I recently started using The Ordinary products, including their cleanser, toner, various serums, emulsion, and two moisturisers, and I couldn't be happier with the results! After just three weeks of consistent use, my skin looks clearer and brighter than it has in a long time.The cleanser is gentle yet effective, leaving my skin feeling fresh without any dryness. The toner adds a lovely layer of hydration and preps my skin perfectly for the serums. Each serum has its unique benefits, and I love how they feel on my skin—lightweight and absorbing quickly. The emulsion adds a nice boost of moisture, and the two moisturisers provide excellent hydration without being heavy or greasy.What truly stands out is the incredible value for money these products offer. They deliver professional-quality results at a fraction of the price of many high-end brands. I wholeheartedly recommend The Ordinary to anyone looking to enhance their skincare routine. My skin has transformed, and I can't wait to see how it continues to improve!

title: Long time customer, not treated as one
body: I have been a long time customer of The Ordinary and have always had good experiences with the product.I placed an order last night (less than 24 hours ago) and then this morning I see an email showing one of the products I ordered for the large size (which I ordered) for the price of the small size.I called customer service and explained and asked if they can adjust the price of that particular product, The rep Yashneet said that there is nothing she can do, I can return the project but I would have to pay the shipping. I explained I was not dissatisfied with the product but just wanted an adjustment on the price. She said I had 1 one hour from the time I placed the order to cancel. Again I did not want to cancel I was asking them to honor the new price. Asked for a manager and she said they will tell me the same. Why have managers if they can't override. I am very frustrated, have been a long time customer and it just feels like customer service is not for the customer. Then I asked if I oder the discounted product today can she give me free shipping since I only wanted that item and they have a $25 minimum for free shipping, again pushback , said they don't have a code of free shipping.I am very disappointed in the company.

title: NO DELIVERY AFTER 2 WEEKS
body: I ordered a product on their website 2 weeks ago now and it still hasn't been delivered (order number 19181411). According to UPS tracking it's been in a UPS (UK) depot for 12 days waiting for me to collect it! I have never agreed to collect it! The agreement when I ordered was to have it delivered to my home address. I've contacted The Ordinary's customer service team and they've basically washed their hands of it and asked me to sort it out with UPS myself which I will not do. UPS is The Ordinary's courier and they must take responsibility and sort out any problems with deliveries. It's not my responsibility. UPDATE: Finally received the delivery 3 weeks late with foc product by way of apology/service recovery.

title: Disappointing customer service and lack of knowledge
body: I went into their Square One Mississauga store and had the worst customer service. One other customer in the store and 3 associates. All 3 associates were standing around the cash register talking loudly about someone they work with (in a very unprofessional way). I had to approach them to ask for some assistance. I went in with the intention of buying a whole new skin regimen. I asked about moisturizer and said I had dry skin. The associate showed me one moisturizer, then corrected himself to say no, it was the other moisturizer that is for dry skin. I had zero confidence in his knowledge of the products, so didn’t ask about anything else. Very disappointed in my experience as I was going into the store specifically because a friend had had such a great experience there a couple of months ago.

title: Extremely Frustrating Order Experience
body: I am writing to express my deep frustration with my recent attempts to place an order on The Ordinary website. For the past two days, I have repeatedly encountered the same error message: "We're sorry that your order could not be placed. This probably happened due to a high order volume or temporary connection errors......."I reached out to your customer support team via online chat for assistance, and unfortunately, the experience has been far from satisfactory:1. I was advised to contact my card issuer/bank to have transactions on your website unflagged. This suggestion is quite surprising, as I have used this card successfully on numerous other websites for the past five years without any issues.2. After trying to use a second card, my attempts were blocked due to multiple payment attempts within a short period. I was informed that I now have to wait at least 24 hours before trying again or using a different account and payment method. This is not only inconvenient but also frustrating.3. I have attempted every possible solution, including creating a new account, adding new payment methods, and even placing an order as a guest - all to no avail.This has been an incredibly time-consuming and disappointing experience. I have never encountered such difficulties with online shopping on any other platform. I strongly urge you to review and update your current system to align with modern standards, as the existing processes are causing significant inconvenience to customers.

title: Most useless kind of online shop
body: Most useless kind of online shopI chose some products, placed an order and paid. Then I apparently had to wait for more than 10 days to get this order at my front door. Ok, why not.The problem is that the courier, UPS in my case, pretended they had tried to deliver it on some random day, but no one was home. Of course there were even several persons home on that day, and weirdly enough none of us heard the doorbell ringing, and I didn't get any call either on that day.When contacting UPS by email and somewhat on the phone (not an easy task), they can't find my tracking, or my package in their system. So I decided to write an email to The Ordinary to get help. They waited for a confortable full week before answering. First they copied some kind of WikiHow article to explain how to use the UPS website, so I knew they hadn't read my initial email, where I explained everything I had done, including all that. After answering them that, they finally tell me the package had just been sent back towards them this very day, very convenient indeed. They are now just starting a refund process after I had to threaten them with their own legal documents. It will be at least 8 business days, and I have the feeling I might need to send a few more emails to get it done properly.So if you're interesting into getting a fake package sent to your place, and never see an inch of it, The Ordinary is the company you need!

title: Products are great, but...
body: Products are great, but it would be nice for them to arrive not damaged!I have placed 2 orders with the ordinary just before Christmas and in the last month or so. The first order which was a gift arrived and the boxes were severely damaged. I wrote to customer services who explained they don't replace products unless I am prepared to send them back, which I understood but it's not ideal when its at the customers inconvenience and a few days before I needed to wrap them up and give them to a friend as a gift!I then ordered my usual cleanser and some other bits in February. My cleanser arrived and the bottle had burst at the end and had leaked all over the packaging and other products. I wrote to customer services on 29/02 and have still heard nothing and have no cleanser to use. In the end, I have taped up the end of the bottle and I'm using it as I don't really want to go out to buy a new cleanser but its beside the point, in 2 weeks I still haven't had my issue resolved. So if you don't mind your products arriving battered or damaged then you'll be fine as the products are great. But I'm seriously considering another brand..

title: I don't get the hype
body: CeraVe is often recommended as the go to brand for dry - sensitive skin.The only product I have liked is the oil based cleanser.The eye cream which is deemed suitable for eczema prone skin actually made it worse. It is very thin and not moisturising at all.The hydrating cream cleanser is ok if I use it now and again but it is stripping if I use it everyday and does have chemically smell. If you are based in the UK B&M have a cheaper alternative which is a much nicer formula.I have tried various moisturisers. Not one of them improved my dry skin and when my skin was extra sensitive. It actually stung when I applied it. I had to reapply throughout the day.There are far far better products out there.I recommend Curel eye cream if you have dry sensitive skin. They do other products too but I haven't tried them.K Beauty products are also very good and reasonable.For something high end I recommend, Dr Jart, Biossance and Drunk Elephant - the Bora Barrier Repair is amazing for very dry skin. It is my winter go to.

title: My saviour
body: I only review when something astounds me or really annoys me. CeraVe astounds me. Here's my story...a year or so ago, my partner for 24 years started changing, you ladies will know, disappointing instances that were not your fault, if you know what I mean, visits getting fewer and fewer, then, suddenly 7 months ago he left me. After a while, I took a good look at myself, sun damaged crepey skin, galloping to my 78th birthday. Nothing I used helped, any results were very temporary. So my last resort, I bought CeraVe moisturiser. Blimey, it works, I'm not looking like a 20 year old, but my damaged skin is smooth, moisturised, and yes, looking nice again. My ex will never see it, but somebody who will appreciate it will, hopefully soon. I can't thank CeraVe enough. I use it at least twice a day, all over and I can't praise it highly enough. Now I look in the mirror and think......he should have waited!

title: I wouldn't recommend this product to…
body: I wouldn't recommend this product to anyone. I developed an autoimmune disease in my 50s which caused my skin to break out in bumps and sores on my face. CeraVe facial wash was recommended as my daily cleanser. I used it for over a year not realizing it was the cleanser making my face even worse! My face felt slimy and greasy and I had bumps all over. My face looked "ruddy" with enlarged pores and overall just a mess. One day the store was out of my cleanser so I tried another brand. My face immediately cleared up! It's smooth, my pores have decreased in size. My daughter who is an estetician told me a while ago NOT to use CeraVe and that customers come to her with extremely clogged pores in which she extracts "goo" I will never use this again. I wish I would have listened to her sooner!

title: CeraVe is counterproductive for sensitive skin.
body: I have used this product off and on for a few years... however, i noticed while using the moisturizer cream that epilating my legs, i end up with more ingrowns and strawberry legs...i originally got it because i have dry legs... and figured this would work during the winter itches... well, this didn't turn out as well as planned... as i found my self compulsively applying more and more...i have sensitive skin on many places of my body and face... so i thought this would help with my pits... as it would end up feeling irritated after shaving... after a few months after i stopped using them on my pits they are not only irritated but super itchy for the first day...i only got negative effects from this product... and i am thinking of tossing the rest of the jar as it has only caused more issues...i read on other posts Aveeno may be a better alturnative... i may resort to that.Thanks CeraVe for giving me drier skin than i already had.

title: Hydrating mineral sunscreen SPF 50
body: My sister bought the broad spectrum SPF 50 face sunscreen at target. She went home, use it.. But her faces looked blue. Keep in mind, she is dark skinned. So she used it again, it looked blue.. yes, blue. So if you don’t know, now yk that a good thump rule of sunscreen is to use your two fingers to measure the amount. So at this point, she realized that this sunscreen made her changed colour. She gave it to me, but the reason why I took it, it’s because I wear makeup so it wouldn’t show. As I said, the thump rule of sunscreen is the two finger thingy thingy and that would be a lot. My sister doesn’t do the thump rule thou, so it could rub out easily. I kid you not, all of my face is literally covered in white cast.(I wish I could upload a photo) I have to use my wash cloth and wipe my hairline, lips, jawline and eyebrow. I have to then try to wipe it with a full force (my hands) but obviously it’s gonna still give you a white case look. As I said, I wear makeup. So I didn’t had a problem using this product but- not everyone wears makeup! I don’t know if it’s okay on light skin or white skin but not for brown/dark skin people. If you gonna wear makeup under the product, ig u can purchase it🤷🏾‍♀️

title: Love their PM Face Moisturizer, Rough & Bumpy Skin, and Eye Repair Cream
body: I love that this brand can be found in the grocery store. I love that regardless of which product I am using, none of them are greasy, and none of them irritate my sensitive skin. I swear by CeraVe Rough & Bumpy Skin, as I have KP, especially on my legs and arms. The Rough & Bumpy Skin lotion help reduce the bumps to make my skin soft and smooth to the touch. I also faithfully use the PM Facial Moisturizer, and Eye Repair. My husband and daughter use the AM Facial Moisturizer with SPF, which was highly recommended by my daughter's dermatologist.

title: Foaming facial cleaner: HORRIBLE
body: BAD THINGS:1. First of all I used this as a facial cleanser at night and end the morning and it worked out HORRIBLY!! My face broke out and was all red and dry. I thought that maybe it had a bad reaction to my skincare/makeup but NOPE! When I didn't put anything on except this (because it was a Sunday) the same thing happened!2. It turns out that CeraVe tests it's products on animals which is a HUGE no for me, but unfortunately I figured that out after I purchased it. It is now in the garbage can waiting for the dump truck to arrive. I told all my friends/family about it and they are now not buying it again due to that reason.3. The foaming facial cleaner doesn't even foam! It just kind of bubbles up in your hands but leaves most of it in the same gooey state, which doesn't really go into your skin very well. I had to purchase a soap-foamer on amazon because it was so sticky. (Link to it at the bottom)4. After using this my face was still oily and not clean, so I noticed no visible difference except that my fake broke out and turned red.GOOD THINGS:1. Very attractive packaging2. The pump doesn't come off like other soaps sometimes doTIPS FOR Y'ALL:For those who have sensitive skin (like me and children) I think you should make a CeraVe KIDS version that is not as strong and is gentler.SOAP FOAMER:(I couldn't add a link but it is called a MARSHMALLOW WHIP MAKER on amazon)

title: Astounded by these products!
body: Astounded by these products!! After using tropic skincare for the best part of a year, I developed horrific acne around my chin and jaw line. My skin was so congested, angry and red I had to stop using. I then went into a prescription cream from the doctor, but needed to find a gentle, water based cleanser and moisturiser. My sister recommended cerave products. I ordered the hydrating cleanser, smoothing cleanser and moisturiser. I also ordered the retinol serum and hydration serum..within one week of using these my skin is clearing up so quickly!! My skin feels so clean, glowing, small pores and so smooth! I am so amazed at this as nothing I’ve ever used before had made my skin look this good. I use the smoothing cleanser only once per week as it contains salicylic acid (the best ingredient for unclogging pores!!) and I use the hydrating cleanser morning and night, followed by the serum and moisturiser and I could not be happier. I’m now thinking of ditching the prescription cream, as I think the only problem was congested skin caused by build up of product from tropic products.

title: 10/10 Would definitely recommend!
body: Recently my skin got infected ( I have eczema and sensitive skin ). After the infection healed, some red patches remained on my skin and they were itching like crazy, sometimes even cracking up and bleeding. I asked the pharmacist for advice on which moisturiser I should use, and they recommended Cerave Face and Body lotion so I gave it a try! title: Used Cerave when I lived in the USA...
body: Used Cerave when I lived in the USA, liked it because I was oily and acne-prone. Live in UK now and ordered a bunch of Cerave for a pretty penny and regretting it. I don't know if the formulation's different. I'd been using Aveeno instead for a few years no problem, ran out, got the Cerave, and my skin has never been more dry.Cerave knows how to create a problem in order to "fix it" (makes me dry during the day, leading the consumer to apply more, and the cycle repeats). Only been using it for a week and my mouth is cracking at the sides and my skin feels tight.I recommend going for lotions that don't have a mile-long list of ingredients. Too expensive to be wrecking my skin. Since I had success in the past I feel bad giving 1 star, but there's no redeeming quality of this at its price-point, so it is what it is.

I'm very disappointed.What's even more baffling is, I posted this same review on their official website but they moderated it and refused to publish it. So if you are seeing this, think twice.

title: Love some products, hate others
body: I love the tinted serum and the concealer—my two favorite products. I do think that the products are a bit overpriced, but I love that I’m paying for quality, non-toxic products. I also wish they would offer promos or sales—which they never do and even admit as much. I also wish they offered customers an option (refund, discount, alternative product, etc.) to make things right if customers aren’t happy or satisfied with a product. They also state this openly—no refunds etc. I just wish there was a guarantee for them, especially since they are so pricey. Most recently I tried the Targeted Skin-Tightening Concentrate, which claims to visibly smooth expression, lines in five minutes. I found this to be a false claim— the product did not do any kind of immediate tightening at all. I’m sure there are long-term benefits because of the ingredients in the product, but I purchased it for the instant tightening effect which it does not do. I’m very disappointed in this particular product and upset that I wasted $50 on a product that doesn’t do what it claims.

title: The products never arrived
body: I am extremely disappointed with my experience.I spent over $200+ on products I was genuinely excited to receive, but my package was delivered to the wrong address. The tracking information clearly shows delivery to a different location, which I can verify through the carrier tracking number.Instead of immediately resolving the issue or filing a claim with the carrier, I was asked to provide a copy of my personal ID in order to receive either a refund or a replacement. I find this request inappropriate and unnecessary, especially considering that this is a delivery error - not an identity issue.As a customer in the United States, I understand that consumer protection standards require the merchant to ensure proper delivery. When a package is delivered to the wrong address, it is considered “not received,” and the responsibility lies with the seller, not the buyer.Having to prove my identity for a package that was misdelivered - despite clear tracking evidence - feels excessive and frankly disappointing. I have never experienced anything like this before.I sincerely hope this situation will be resolved properly and professionally.

title: There are better companies that actually care about their customers
body: My experiences in dealing with Typology teams has been abysmal. The owner as well, as I cc’d him directly through email on the issues I have experienced with his team.The company itself if penny wise, pound foolish and needs a real lesson in professionalism with a focus on customer retention. You know, the customers that keep them in business?I have been a customer for almost two years. My first interaction with their team, a few months ago, was over concealer being mis-packaged/labeled. I brought the issue to customer service with photos to demonstrate that what I received was the wrong color inside of the correctly labeled/ordered tube. After weeks of frustrating back and forth, proving that they had made a mistake in manufacturing/packaging, the best they could do was send me a $17 discount on my NEXT order. Not a replacement, as they should have done, a discount on my NEXT expenditure. All I really wanted an assurance that my next order would be correct, that they looked into the issue and if it wasn’t, they would prepay for return shipping. They wouldn’t do that. If the next order received had the same issue, I would be responsible to pay that return shipping cost.I took another chance and placed another order almost two weeks ago. It was supposed to arrive in three days. I received a few emails telling me my order was delayed and they were working on it. I looked into the reason for myself after the last email with the tracking provided. The tracking showed that a label was created but the package sat in their Pennsylvania warehouse and never made it to the post office. Someone in their warehouse made a mistake. I alerted the company and asked if they could send out a new order express shipping. They ignored that request and sent me now a $20 credit towards my NEXT purchase with a three month expiration date. An expiration date? My order is now on its way now, presumably by horseback because it won’t be received for another week, from Pennsylvania. I’m in Florida.Had they done the right thing here, and sent out a new order express, as I requested and what they should have done on their own once realizing THEIR MISTAKE, this customer would have remained a customer. I won’t be receiving the order I placed on 12/21 until 1/5 and AGAIN it’s not coming from France, it’s was shipped two days ago from Pennsylvania. Pennsylvania NOT FRANCE people, despite their claims AND they chose the slowest and cheapest shipping method available. Penny wise… in other words cheapskates.The irony is that my order was two dollars shy of their recent Christmas offer-spend $80 and receive free EXPRESS shipping.As far as their products go my opinions are mixed. My first order was close to $200, using their diagnostic tool. All of what was recommended, serums, face wash, cleansing oil, etc., were a bust and don’t live up to their claims with the exception of the 9 ingredient moisturizer, (which works well and has a light texture, except it isn’t non-comedogenic).The concealer works well and blends well but the tube packaging is wasteful and messy. A lot of the product that you’re paying for winds up oozing out if the cap isn’t put back on immediately or tight enough and then wasted. It also gets wasted because you can’t get it all out of the tube, like you could with a traditional bottle. Terrible design. Wasteful.Fingers crossed that this order for concealer will be the correct color inside of the correctly labeled tube and I can be done with two bit, low class, sorry to say sh!t show, Typology.Such a shame.Update 1/14/26Typology has now made matters worse by resorting to LYING to the public here about the mistake that they made in their reply to my honest review. They are now blaming the post office for their mistake while trying to make me appear as unreasonable. Truly pathetic. I’m unable to affix a screenshot here of the tracking to show that my order sat on someone’s desk or in someone’s car for eight days in PENNSYLVANIA NOT FRANCE until I alerted them on 12/30 and it was only then, that they brought it to the post office BUT here’s the copy of tracking to prove they’re now lying. They also never provided me with any “solutions”. New low.USPS“Your item arrived at our LANCASTER PA DISTRIBUTION CENTER origin facility on December 30, 2025 at 11:50 am. The item is currently in transit to the destination-• Arrived at USPS Regional Origin FacilityLANCASTER PA DISTRIBUTION CENTERDecember 30, 2025, 11:50 am• Accepted at USPS Origin FacilityNORTHAMPTON, PA 18067December 30, 2025, 10:35 am• Shipping Label Created, USPS Awaiting ItemNORTHAMPTON, PA 18067December 22, 2025, 4:27 pm

I alerted the company and asked if they could send out a new order express shipping. They ignored that request and sent me now a $20 credit towards my NEXT purchase with a three month expiration date. An expiration date? My order is now on its way now, presumably by horseback because it won’t be received for another week, from Pennsylvania. I’m in Florida.Had they done the right thing here, and sent out a new order express, as I requested and what they should have done on their own once realizing THEIR MISTAKE, this customer would have remained a customer. I won’t be receiving the order I placed on 12/21 until 1/5 and AGAIN it’s not coming from France, it’s was shipped two days ago from Pennsylvania. Pennsylvania NOT FRANCE people, despite their claims AND they chose the slowest and cheapest shipping method available. Penny wise… in other words cheapskates.The irony is that my order was two dollars shy of their recent Christmas offer-spend $80 and receive free EXPRESS shipping.As far as their products go my opinions are mixed. My first order was close to $200, using their diagnostic tool. All of what was recommended, serums, face wash, cleansing oil, etc., were a bust and don’t live up to their claims with the exception of the 9 ingredient moisturizer, (which works well and has a light texture, except it isn’t non-comedogenic).The concealer works well and blends well but the tube packaging is wasteful and messy. A lot of the product that you’re paying for winds up oozing out if the cap isn’t put back on immediately or tight enough and then wasted. It also gets wasted because you can’t get it all out of the tube, like you could with a traditional bottle. Terrible design. Wasteful.Fingers crossed that this order for concealer will be the correct color inside of the correctly labeled tube and I can be done with two bit, low class, sorry to say sh!t show, Typology.Such a shame.Update 1/14/26Typology has now made matters worse by resorting to LYING to the public here about the mistake that they made in their reply to my honest review. They are now blaming the post office for their mistake while trying to make me appear as unreasonable. Truly pathetic. I’m unable to affix a screenshot here of the tracking to show that my order sat on someone’s desk or in someone’s car for eight days in PENNSYLVANIA NOT FRANCE until I alerted them on 12/30 and it was only then, that they brought it to the post office BUT here’s the copy of tracking to prove they’re now lying. They also never provided me with any “solutions”. New low.USPS“Your item arrived at our LANCASTER PA DISTRIBUTION CENTER origin facility on December 30, 2025 at 11:50 am. The item is currently in transit to the destination-• Arrived at USPS Regional Origin FacilityLANCASTER PA DISTRIBUTION CENTERDecember 30, 2025, 11:50 am• Accepted at USPS Origin FacilityNORTHAMPTON, PA 18067December 30, 2025, 10:35 am• Shipping Label Created, USPS Awaiting ItemNORTHAMPTON, PA 18067December 22, 2025, 4:27 pm

title: Don't be drawn in by Insta influencers
body: UPDATE: Typology kindly got in touch and replaced the lip oil for me, but I will not be travelling with it, just in case! ORIGINAL REVIEW: Lots of the influencers (mostly mom and scandi influencers) I follow on Instagram were raving about Typology and I was drawn in and asked for two products as a gift from my husband for my birthday. I got the lip oil in "powder pink" which I LOVE as a product. It's moisturising and a nice subtle shade. However, I would not get again as the bottle leaks badly. So you can't travel with it. Which honestly is a deal breaker as normally one would take their lip product out for the day. And who wants it all over the inside of their handbag? I now carry mine around in a ziplock bag smeared with product. NOT great. I also got the vitamin C glow drops and those made my skin break out in little red bumps that took months to go away. They did seem to brighten my skin, so a shame. I have sensitive skin, so I wouldn't recommend the glow drops if you also have sensitive skin. Don't fall for the hype - do your own research!!

title: Worst Punitive Customer Service I've Ever Encountered
body: I ordered their serum foundation and concealer to try, as they offer no way to test out shades so I did my best in ordering, thinking "of course they offer exchanges, it would be crazy not to!"Well, reader, they don't offer exchanges. In fact, if you do happen to order the wrong shade of foundation (as most do) you not only have to pay a return fee to send it back, but have to pay shipping again for your new shade that maybe hopefully fits you this time.I asked for a free shipping code or exception to this policy and was denied, so decided that it made the most sense to return both the foundation and the concealer together to then re order both to gain free shipping through their $60 minimum. Nope, can't do that either because now it's been over 14 days since my product was delivered and that's too long to start a return according to French law?? Yes, legitimately, the CSA was citing French law to me in their email responses. The only reason we went past 14 days, by the way, is they take nearly 72 hours to respond to any email I send, essentially running out the clock. My first email was within 4 days of delivery, and the rest of the correspondence took over 10 days to get to where it was "too late to return."I have never in my life encountered customer service policies and application so punitive, mean spirited and ridiculous in my life. FOR A FOUNDATION...something that is notorious for not fitting the user off the bat to no fault of their own. No way to try their product, no samples, nothing in store, yet charging over $80 for a tiny bottle of foundation and concealer with an embarrassing shade range to boot.Typology has 100% lost me as a customer. I wasn't even impressed with how the foundation applied and the concealer creases so badly it's hard to justify either. I'm unable to return it, though, since their policies are from the dark ages. Guess I'll have to take this up with my credit card company.Good luck to you, I hope you order the correct shade from the start!

title: High quality and effective clean products!
body: Excellent experience so far with quality and effectiveness of Typology products. I am 49 with sensitive and dry skin that battles redness. After completing the online questionnaire, I began using the recommended skin care products and found the 7-ingredient oil cleanser, 9-ingredient serum and C40 moisturizer instantly calming, skin less red. After a week, my face was significantly less red and was staying moisturized through the whole day so I ordered the green color corrector and tinted serum and lip oil to add to my routine. All are equally fantastic. The tinted serum is not drying, easy to apply with fingers or brush and gives just enough tint to make my face appear more even in tone. The lip oil gives a nice tint of color and keeps my lips hydrated long after I apply. The mascara is the best “clean” ingredient mascara I have ever used. It does not smear, even with watery eyes, yet is easy to remove. (It is not especially thickening but coats well and stays put, so I use it daily.). All product packaging is attractive and easier to use than most brands I have tried over the years. I particularly like the bottle sizes and shapes. Easy to recycle. The metal tube for the moisturizer requires more care - need to squeeze only from the bottom - but I still prefer it to plastic tubes that are difficult to extract the entire product. Shipping has been very quick and packaging very secure and attractive - all bottles have been wrapped and placed so there is no possibility of breakage or leaks. I cannot comment on customer service because I have been completely satisfied with every purchase so far (I have and use 9 Typology products daily). However, I am incredibly impressed with the quality and efficacy of Typology’s products. Please continue to add to your make-up line. And, it would be great if you made a good mineral SPF.
full_text: High quality and effective clean products! Excellent experience so far with quality and effectiveness of Typology products. I am 49 with sensitive and dry skin that battles redness. After completing the online questionnaire, I began using the recommended skin care products and found the 7-ingredient oil cleanser, 9-ingredient serum and C40 moisturizer instantly calming, skin less red. After a week, my face was significantly less red and was staying moisturized through the whole day so I ordered the green color corrector and tinted serum and lip oil to add to my routine. All are equally fantastic. The tinted serum is not drying, easy to apply with fingers or brush and gives just enough tint to make my face appear more even in tone. The lip oil gives a nice tint of color and keeps my lips hydrated long after I apply. The mascara is the best “clean” ingredient mascara I have ever used. It does not smear, even with watery eyes, yet is easy to remove. (It is not especially thickening but coats well and stays put, so I use it daily.). All product packaging is attractive and easier to use than most brands I have tried over the years. I particularly like the bottle sizes and shapes. Easy to recycle. The metal tube for the moisturizer requires more care - need to squeeze only from the bottom - but I still prefer it to plastic tubes that are difficult to extract the entire product. Shipping has been very quick and packaging very secure and attractive - all bottles have been wrapped and placed so there is no possibility of breakage or leaks. I cannot comment on customer service because I have been completely satisfied with every purchase so far (I have and use 9 Typology products daily). However, I am incredibly impressed with the quality and efficacy of Typology’s products. Please continue to add to your make-up line. And, it would be great if you made a good mineral SPF.

title: ❌ Disappointed and frankly insulted
body: ❌ Disappointed and frankly insulted – Topology SkincareI’ve been a customer of Topology Skincare before, so this experience really caught me off guard.On July 8th, I placed a fairly large order (€650–700 worth of products), which I planned to take with me outside the EU, since the brand doesn’t ship directly to my country. For almost two weeks, I heard nothing. Then on July 14th, I received a generic email saying there might be a delay — which was fine. I understand logistics can get tricky.But when I checked the order status myself a week later and reached out to customer support, I was shocked to be told that my order wasn’t going to be shipped because they suspected I was a reseller.No one contacted me. No one asked me anything. They just made that judgment internally and stayed quiet. And apparently, had I not followed up, I wouldn’t have known anything at all.Frankly, I found that insulting. I’ve ordered from them before — including large quantities — and never had an issue. Accusing a loyal customer of reselling without any proof, and without even bothering to communicate, is not just bad customer service, it’s unacceptable.To be honest, it felt more like they were having stock or fulfilment issues and needed an excuse. And the “reseller” label was a convenient way out.They did issue the refund promptly once I asked, but that’s not the point. The lack of transparency, the silence, and the baseless accusation left a really bad impression. I expected much better from a brand that positions itself as high-end.

title: I bought the Typology Tinted Serum and Concealer
body: I bought the Typology Tinted Serum and Concealer.I sent in a picture; Typology recommended Fair. I got the Fair -- Irish skin kind of fair -- it's too light. Bought the Light, which is really dark, maybe Mexican or Indian skin dark. In most brands, my skin is Light to Medium.I mixed it and that is one nightmare. It does not pour from the bottle. You have to transfer it with the dispenser. Messy, slow, a chore.However, I LOVE the tinted serum. It's great for the skin. It looks like you have nothing on. I'm an older person with dry, dehydrated skin, and this is perfect. It's the only "foundation" I found that does not look like a foundation; does not settle into creases; makes you look great; and looks like you're not wearing anything.I tried the concealer in Light. Way, way to dark. So, got it in Fair. That is perfect. It's an excellent concealer.Again, the tube in comes in is crap. The concealer comes out of there without even pressing the tube.Overall, I love both, the tinted serum and concealer.I think they designed it for you to mix your perfect color, BUT they made it almost impossible to do so. If they either added colors, or changed their making it easier to mix, or come out of that tube, that would be great.

title: Their refund policy and customer service is terrible
body: Without in store trial I purchased the color that best suited me based on the site recommendation. The colors were significantly off, and none of the lighter concealers worked for me. There's zinc powder turned my whole face cakey. And though their foundation in the correct color was delightful and their green color corrector I swear by, I have lost so much money with a foundation, two concealers and a powder that simply didn't work. This is not drugstore stuff. It's not $15 down the drain. It's hundreds. And it's a shame because I won't purchase again just based off of this experience. They are a dime a dozen and there's no reason why a big company or any company trying to get foot in the makeup world should be treating their customers.

title: Does not alert of product stockouts
body: I ordered my second round of Typology products after loving the ruby red lip oil, makeup remover, and azelaic acid serum. This time I got two more lip oils, two face cleansers, the face tint in tan, and a shampoo and scalp scrub duo - only the shampoo was never shipped (6.76 ounces) and instead a "mini" bar was sent out in its place (1.9 ounces) with no alert of the substitution made. When I emailed customer service asking for a refund since I did not receive the right product, they only offered a 30% refund. For a product I paid 100% for.Edit to amend: The shipping note was wrong - they marked that they would send out the 1.9 ounce shampoo bar in the email to me, but actually sent out the full size shampoo. However, when I sent the screenshot of this to the customer service person, I still don’t appreciate their suggested resolution. I am giving 4 stars because I still LOVE their products.

title: Very scummy way of keeping money from the customers
body: I needed to cancel an order right after I ordered something because I realise I made a mistake. I have written them and they said it's too late I need to return the product. I then simply refused accepting the product on DHL's tracking and it was successful, however, the package was wrongly routed on its way back. I asked Typology what they could do in this situation, they said they can send me another product, but without the original package, they cannot refund me. I asked them what if the package is lost? They replied the same thing again although I made it clear that I don't need a new product, this is last week.This week, fortunately the package was delivered back, the customer service contacted me and sent me a link to request a refund, it was the US site, so I asked again, then I realised the link was there the whole time but it was TINY underneath the button of requesting a new product. I clicked on that tiny link to request a refund. I am not sure whether they did it intentionally or not, but they made it unclear that the refund is to be refunded as credit into typology account, so now my money is stuck there. I wrote their customer service again, they told me it is "not possible" to refund me anymore because now the credit is in my typology account.Where is the logic? If the "credit" is in the typology account, it means the money is in the typology account, it is definitely illegal to withhold credit from the customer under EU law, even if I (accidentally) agreed to accept the credit, I should still be able to request it to be transferred into my bank account as cash. I don't really care how good their product is, but such a way of doing business is simply unbelievable. I don't blame the customer service agent, but I think the company isn't ethical at all by the way they deal with the refund of 35 EUR. I cannot even trust them to put good ingredient and sell the products with a reasonable price pointUpdate: I opened a case with paypal and they sent over a fake dhl tracking to show paypal that the product arrived to me on 5.6, this is the worst customer service case I have ever had and it’s a pure fraud. Paypal’s automatic system closed my case so I have to call them to escalate. I am not sure why they want to make a random customer’s life difficult, but at this point it is a fraud case, so I would report it to the EU consumer protection association if I need to in the future.

They replied the same thing again although I made it clear that I don't need a new product, this is last week.This week, fortunately the package was delivered back, the customer service contacted me and sent me a link to request a refund, it was the US site, so I asked again, then I realised the link was there the whole time but it was TINY underneath the button of requesting a new product. I clicked on that tiny link to request a refund. I am not sure whether they did it intentionally or not, but they made it unclear that the refund is to be refunded as credit into typology account, so now my money is stuck there. I wrote their customer service again, they told me it is "not possible" to refund me anymore because now the credit is in my typology account.Where is the logic? If the "credit" is in the typology account, it means the money is in the typology account, it is definitely illegal to withhold credit from the customer under EU law, even if I (accidentally) agreed to accept the credit, I should still be able to request it to be transferred into my bank account as cash. I don't really care how good their product is, but such a way of doing business is simply unbelievable. I don't blame the customer service agent, but I think the company isn't ethical at all by the way they deal with the refund of 35 EUR. I cannot even trust them to put good ingredient and sell the products with a reasonable price pointUpdate: I opened a case with paypal and they sent over a fake dhl tracking to show paypal that the product arrived to me on 5.6, this is the worst customer service case I have ever had and it’s a pure fraud. Paypal’s automatic system closed my case so I have to call them to escalate. I am not sure why they want to make a random customer’s life difficult, but at this point it is a fraud case, so I would report it to the EU consumer protection association if I need to in the future.

title: Logistics and customer service are abysmal
body: Their products are a 5-star for me. However, logistics and customer service are abysmal. I’m writing a review here out of desperation to hear from Typology. I purchased items on June 4, and there has been no progress/movement on the shipment status since then. It is June 15 today as I write this, and the order status still says “Ready for pick up”. I sent a DM on Instagram and did not receive a response. I just wrote a comment on one of the IG posts to see if I’ll get a response. Shipment should not take this long from Paris to Singapore. Lack of communication and poor logistics are usually overlooked by start-ups that suddenly scale and gain success.
