title: SCAM TAILORED FOR USA MARKET
body: DO NOT BUY! Typology is clearly a scam created by a millionaire who wants to make more money, extremely high prices for supposedly clean products with effective active ingredients, but almost nothing is organic, their active ingredients are not stable (Retinol serum without airless pump and/or mild preservatives, even though retinol is known to be VERY unstable, and the lipid-replenishing balm that goes moldy after 2 weeks of use), ridiculously small or half-filled tubes that last less than 2 weeks, in short, this is a brand that invests heavily in marketing and fancy glass bottles but not much about what's inside. .. On top of that, the customer service is appalling: they refuse to give your money back even with photographic evidence of a faulty product like mold. I'm going back to my real French organic brands, which may have less attractive packaging, but at least the quality is there and prices are decent!

title: Feeling and seeing results
body: I'm old enough to have earned skepticism about skin care products, reluctant to believe most of what I read. Still, I decided to try Typology out of curiosity about the skin analysis and specific recommendations. My skin is 73 years old. My goal is to maintain as gracefully as possible. After 3 months of pretty consistent use, I believe not from magic but with steady, regular use combined with other self care like sleep, diet, and a peaceful mind, my skin feels smoother, firmer and redness is quieter. I have been using serums P63 (firming) and A34 (wrinkles/firmness) and C40 (moisturizer and redness relief). Based on my own results and Typology's prompt shipment and polite follow up, I will choose other items in the future.

title: recently purchased several products…
body: recently purchased several products from Typology, including the tinted serum, concealer, mascara, lip oil, and lipstick. Unfortunately, I was quite disappointed with most of the items, except for the mascara and lipstick.The lipstick is genuinely impressive—hydrating, comfortable, and wears beautifully throughout the day. The mascara is also a standout: light, natural-looking, and easy to apply without clumping.However, the tinted serum was a major letdown. I initially ordered the medium shade, which turned my skin noticeably orange and looked cakey. I tried the light shade next, but that ended up patchy and uneven. I have generally good skin, and I’ve never experienced this kind of reaction before—even with other skincare-makeup hybrids like the IT Cosmetics CC Cream. The concealer had the same issue: patchy application and poor longevity. After just a few hours, my complexion looked uneven and fatigued.Given how Typology markets these products as a fusion of skincare and makeup, I expected better performance and skin compatibility. Sadly, that promise didn’t hold true in my experience. I really wanted to love these, but overall, I’m disappointed.

title: Great idea however…
body: Great idea. French product, utilitarian packaging, no fuss at all label….however,Product is a “serum” used in face care after, or before, a softer. this one is tinted; like a bb cream but ..another foundation type- more sticky. Product does not smell so good after a few weeks so I am concerned about preservatives. Made in France but shipped from Pennsylvania. Maybe the glass part is made in France. Way too expensive yet - you do not need much. Not sure what the product does - coverage seems to fade, can’t figure out why. Maybe more of a tinted base.. yet it dries to matte finish. Maybe you need to work it in more …?? Searched ingredients - no silicone and it’s vegan. No patent yet a list of random ingredients good for skin but a no-show of preservatives, emulsifing agents…The glom on to “french” is a move from “K” products - yet the stronghold on ‘SERUM’ is great marketing and actually a great idea. I will take my usual serum and try something else …..

title: Nice products shame about the courier
body: I ordered these products after seeing them on YouTube. I bought a set which consisted of the Tinted Serum in medium, the concealer in medium and the lip oil in Powder Pink plus I had a free bottle of eye make-up remover and a small make-up bag. The tinted serum was quite dark (wish I'd read the reviews first) so I mixed it with my Sculptured Beauty Base which was fine. I have since discovered that if I dab the serum onto my face and then blend with a brush it's not actually as dark as I thought and I like it now. It gives my face a healthy glow. The concealer again is a bit dark but my main complaint is the tube - it ouzes out of the tube and is very wasteful. I shall try and decant it into one of those small travel jars. The tinted lip oil is gorgeous I love it and the bottle is small enough to put in a handbag (doesn't last all day). The eye make-up remover is very nice. I will probably order again but what puts me off is the courier they use - DPD who are next to useless. I changed my delivery day and they kept trying to deliver it beforehand sending me e-mails to say that no-one was in and at one point telling me it had been delivered when it hadn't. I was in the next time they delivered it but it was still not the date I had asked them to deliver it! Bit of a nightmare to be quite honest.

Given the hype surrounding this product online, I expected much better. Instead, I am now dealing with the aftermath of a terrible breakout and will need to see a dermatologist.I am extremely disappointed in this experience and the lack of prompt customer service. I expect an immediate resolution regarding my refund.* I posted my pictures and experience on Reddit for everyone’s reference*

title: I really wanted to love Typology
body: I really wanted to love Typology, but I’m disappointed with how my order cancellation was handled. I realized there was a mistake with the shades just a few hours after placing my order and immediately submitted a cancellation request.It feels frustrating knowing the order could’ve been stopped in time, especially since it hadn’t been processed yet. I was really hoping for quicker communication and better customer service in this situation.I still like the concept of the brand, but I’m hesitant to order again because of the lack of responsiveness when I needed help the most.

title: Customs charges, Vat and handling fees…
body: Great products but I was left feeling very angry at the customs charges and additional handling fees of £42!!! Having ordered from Typology previously, I hadn't paid any vat or customs charges so felt comfortable that this would be the case with my next order. It wasn't. Customer service just pointed me in the direction of their terms of sale hidden at the bottom of their website. Be aware that the price listed on the product website might not be what you actually pay. I won't be a return customer again. They need to improve.

title: Surprised and very happy!
body: I just discovered Typology and have been extremely pleased with this brand. I love that it is vegan, with minimal and eco-friendly packaging, and that it scores so well on Yuka. My skin also loves these products, especially in the dry winter. I have been using the 9-ingredient moisturizer, hyaluronic serum, vit c serum, and under-eye serum, and I have seen a noticeable difference in my skin's texture and radiance after a month of using these products. Will definitely be purchasing more!

title: concealer in the light shade
body: I ordered the concealer in the light shade as I always do from all brands.The description of the shade matches to what I need. When I received the concealer I realized that it is a very dark orange and there is no way anyone with light completion could use it. Especially under the eyes where you need to brighten. The description is misleading. I'm very disappointed as I was excited to try this brand and hopefully switch to more products. They do not offer any money back so I'm just wasted £30 on a product that I will never be able to use and I still don't have s concealer to use. Stay away from this brand. Thre are better brands you can use.

title: I fell into the marketing trap with…
body: I fell into the marketing trap with this company and i dont like writing negative reviews but I feel like this is necessary for people to see this brand is a scam. After purchasing almost $300 worth of product, I received the products and realized they are not the magic workers, nothing speacial really and I could live with that. However, the shades are absolutely off for the concealer and tinted serum. I went for medium as my skin tone and they came darker and oranger. When I approached to the customer service, they simply said no returns or exchanges. I am not shopping this brand again and it’s a bummer because I really thought this could be my go to make up brand - not with this kind of customer service.

title: In Love with These Products! Just a Few Suggestions
body: Since I started using these products, I’m totally in love! My skin feels so much better, and they don’t make my face look cakey or settle into fine lines. I already have several products—some for acne, face cleansing, the tinted makeup, and the glow drops—and I love all of them!My only small complaint is that the tinted makeup in shade #2 is a bit too pink for me, so I think I might need to try shade #1. I’ll probably mix them together to see how that works. It would be really helpful if you offered small samples to help match the right shade to different skin tones.Also, it would be amazing if you could offer refills for the tinted makeup and other products that come in glass containers—or maybe even a trade-in program for them.Thanks for creating such wonderful products!

title: My order was lost in shipment last year…
body: My order was lost in shipment last year and I started the customer service ticket and then forgot about it. A whole year later, I reached out again, and they swiftly issued my credit. I love Typology. I had stocked up on their tinted serum and I finally ran out. I replenished this product, as well as adding their cream, Vitamin C serum, and cleansing oil. And with my order I received a gift of reusable cleansing pads! They are amazing and I hope the USA President does not impose tariffs so I can still afford this amazing product line. There is nothing as clean and beautifully packaged as Typology in the USA.

title: Lash and brow serum no bueno
body: I saw reviews raving about it the eyelash and eyebrow serum. I used it at first and was seeing good results over time I noticed I kept getting burning eyes. I stopped using the serum on my lashes and the burning went away. I started to use it again and then I started getting inflamed tear ducts and it looked like pink eye. I stopped using it again and my symptoms went away. I will never use this product again. Feels like a scam to me

title: Clean Beauty that’s good for you and the environment
body: I have ordered a few times and shipping is fast from the time I order to when I receive, the items are packed with care and always shipped in recyclable packing. I’ve ordered the green tint, a couple serums and concealers and another tinted serum and a nice lip oil. The color matching to my skin is not perfect with the concealers and face tint, but they still work well and they provide a nice light coverage that doesn’t sink into fine lines. I think having more color options to their tinted products would be good, but I’m sure the company will add more in time. I would order again as I try to only buy clean beauty products. No added fragrance is another plus.

title: Typology is a marketing scam
body: Typology is a marketing scam. They are inflated with hundreds of fake reviews across multiple forums. The cleanser caused a major allergic reaction that required topical steroids and the tinted serum in shade 2 (light) was orange on my skin. I typically wear light-medium shades and opted into shade 2 (light) for winter and it is way too dark. I saw the option to click a link in the order delivery email to "Return or Exchange" but turns out that there are NO RETURNS OR EXCHANGES even for unused product (I was afraid to try other products after the allergic reaction to the cleanser). Customer service was not helpful.

title: I happened upon this skin care as I was…
body: I happened upon this skin care as I was looking for natural skincare for menopausal skin. My skin has been really dry and extremely sensitive for several months. Nothing I tried helped. Even the so called natural products cause breakouts or allergic reactions. Several products even caused chemical burns. I was extremely skeptical about trying these products but I decided to give them a try. I am so happy I did. My skin is super hydrated, and I have not had any allergic reactions whatsoever. My skin tone is more even and hydrated and my make up glides on smoothly. I am so happy I have this skin care a try. I absolutely love these products and I am loving my skin.

title: Amazing products.
body: Most of my life (now in my mid-50s) I have rarely worn makeup. I don’t like how it feels, the effort it takes to put on, or how it sits on my skin. I’ve tried it all from creams to powders to baked. I decided to try Typology’s tinted line and I am in love. It is so natural and just leaves me looking a bit fresher. It is fast to apply and so nourishing. I use it daily. The first day I wore it to work I had 3 people stop and say “you look so great today!” I loved it so much I purchased skincare, which is also wonderful. I cannot recommend this brand enough.

title: Fill out the skin questionnaire, and get their prescription - no regrets!
body: I so appreciate the questionnaire on the skin issues one is having, and the prescription for what could fix it. I love this company and their products. Everything I've tried has been superb. Love the lack of plastic packaging and glass bottles. I have aging skin, spots, wrinkles - all the problems one gets north of 50. So few skin care regimens successfully tackle them. The prescription Typology gave me from me honestly answering the questionnaire is outstanding. I bought nearly all the recommended products. I'm only on day 3, and I already see brighter, clearer skin and radiance emerging. It's expensive, but so worth it, and I feel I am worth having terrific skin. So are you. I also very much appreciate the gifts that came with my purchase. Again, environmentally conscious and super useful.

title: Absolutely over the moon
body: Absolutely over the moon.I was a little nervous about ordering from a brand I wasn't too familiar with beyond some influencer posts - but I am absolutely thrilled with the products.I currently use their Exfoliating Cleanser - the scent is refreshing & lovely - and a little goes a LONG way. My skin glows after a few weeks of using this & it's also great to use for a double cleanse on days I wear a full face of makeup. It's not too harsh - and my skin never feels dry after using.I also purchased three serums, the Wrinkle Serum, Radiance & Eye Serum - and am pleasantly surprised with them all. They work well - and my skin ( typically sensitive - have to be very careful of new products) has reacted well. My ONLY complaint is the dropper doesn't always fill up with product & I wish it was a larger bottle.I also purchased the skin tint - which, initially following the online skin match - got a color that was WAY too dark, and when I contacted customer service they promptly helped me get the right color. I really like this for summer - light-weight & hydrating. However, it feels like the bottles aren't filled with product and the dropper applicator leaves a lot to be desired on this. Not sure I will repurchase.Overall - I really like the line & suggest it to anyone looking to try something new at an affordable price point for more luxury feeling products.

title: Obsessed
body: I’m 58 years old, and I have never found a tinted moisturizer or foundation that I like, let alone love. Until now. Typology’s tinted serum (with an added dash of glow drops) not only makes my skin look radiant, it makes my skin feel like silk. I use the tinted lip oil in Ruby Red on my lips and cheeks. The texture is not thick or sticky, it blends easily and looks like a healthy glow. I also love the tinted concealer with the caffeine. It brightens my face and makes me look refreshed. I’m obsessed with this brand.

title: Very disappointed with the product
body: Very disappointed with the product I received and since typology doesn't have a returns policy, I have sadly wasted my money. I ordered the "T33 - Color Corrector for Redness" and I was excited for the product especially given the claims but it has been about 10 days since I received it and I cannot get the product to work - whenever I apply it, it just looks patchy and "separates" like it wants to pill off your skin.I first thought it was interacting with my sunscreen underneath but I tried it with other sunscreens too and then, at a complete loss, I tried it without any sunscreen on either (even though I wear sunscreen everyday and I would have no use for this product if I couldn't wear it over sunscreen) and it still looked patchy and bad. I tried tapping it in gently, then I tried swiping it on, and I even tried to apply it with a brush or a beauty blender, and none of it worked.I don't have bad skin and I don't have any texture or dry patches on my skin for this product to stick to and it still looks very visible and bad. I am sure for someone with more problematic skin it would be even worse. I don't like wearing makeup and I was hoping I could use this to camouflage some of the rosiness in my skin but it's so noticeable and bad on my skin that I'd rather just use a bit of concealer instead and it would be less noticeable!I am really disappointed because I really wanted to like this product and I was impressed by it being a skincare product first and colour corrector second. Sadly the performance is really sub-par so I'll have to buy either the chanel or givenchy green colour correctors which I've tried in the past and seemed to work much better.I've given my review one extra star for the fact that I really appreciated the eco-friendly packaging they used and also for the fact that I don't really have a problem with the company, just with this one product I've tried. Sadly, there's no way to review an individual product on the typology website so I've had to write a review here so that others may be aware that this product is definitely not worth it in my opinion.

title: i would put minus 5 starts
body: no pleasure to visit physical shop at all.hi to everybody, i would wish to share with my visiting this shop, what was totally stressful for me.first of all its my personal opinion without intention to hurt somebody or devalue merchant.i come to shop to return article, as mentioned at sefora site, i printed bill, did not expect any problems.plus i planned to buy xmas gift for high price, but.... " funny time " started at cassia.p.s I did already complain about the rude behavior of the manager at Sephora to official site. They did NOTHING, only txt me " we r sorry".the story is:I came to return blush I ordered online. At first, one manager couldn't figure out the purchase date when looking at the receipt I received from Sephora. For half an hour, three other people couldn't figure out what was written on my receipt ( what has been send to me by this merchant) !!!After more than 40 minutes, they finally sorted out the receipt and managed to read the date. It's worth noting that the entire conversation was polite, until another lady with a handkerchief around her neck jumped up and asked if I'd made the purchase from the same account. Naturally, I'd paid with a virtual card from Bank. I'd brought my bank card to received refund, which this lady with the handkerchief snatched from my hands without my permission!!!!!!!My card is private property, and it has a 3numbers of secure defending code, which she disclosed in violation of personal data protection laws. What's this? Who gave this woman the right to behave like this? Who gave her the right to grab my bank card and examine all the personal information on it?????? Even though, legally, she had no right to do this! And on camera, too. So, she disclosed all my personal information without my consent, inviting other salespeople to look at information at my card. And she ignored my request to give me my card back immediately!!!! What if I tell her that taking possession of and disclosing card data without the cardholder's consent is against law. That women told she is the main in shop, and she does what she wants ( when i asked to invite top manager). plus that women was talking rude, This lady spoke cynically and categorically, although she was unable to tell me the requested paragraphs of the return conditions, confirmed by documents.after such abuse to me ( by my opinion), i have no wish and pleasure to visit live shop.plus to all some other managers do nothing to help to find correct product, they just staying by group without any steps to customers.Shame to merchantp.s its my personal opinion without intention to hurt somebody.as positive, some girls managers r nice at this shop, and helpful ( to be objective) .but at live shop they never give even any small gifts, even for samples of perfume u must nearly beg them ( at site written, i have rights to have up to 5 samples for each order). in reality manages in shop decide by themselves how many to give to u and dont care what is mentioned at merchant site.highly not recommendafter this terrible behavior of manager, and describing this situation to my bank , my bank adviced me exchanged my bank card, what i did.after this visit i was in total stress during few days

title: No customer help if item arrives not as described
body: I would have loved to try it but i lost $250 because the incorrect merchandise was delivered to me. I got a box with packing material and 2 samples but not the item i ordered. On contacting customer service, i was told sorry there’s nothing that can be done, the item has been delivered (see pictures) they apparently can’t check their inventory, shipping weight or anything else to resolve my issue. Just google “I received an empty box from Sephora” and see how many hits you get. I don’t recommend ordering on line at all because you have no recourse if you receive the wrong item or your order does not ship with the correct merchandise. This is a ridiculous policy. They also do not use a normal shipper so none of the information like package weight or size is available on the package or otherwise.Use jomashop, maxaroma, ecosmetics, ulta or one of the other trusted retailers

title: I wanted to share my disappointment…
body: I wanted to share my disappointment with a recent visit to the Les Halles store. My friend and I approached a staff member named Anisa. While I speak French, my friend doesn’t, so we chose to speak in English to be clear and inclusive. Anisa responded by muttering something under her breath and rolling her eyes, which made us feel unwelcome and judged for being non-locals.Her attitude was dismissive and unprofessional, and unfortunately, this wasn’t an isolated case most of the staff seemed uninterested in helping or engaging with visitors. What should have been a fun and cultural experience turned into an uncomfortable one.I can’t recommend visiting based on this experience. If you're looking for friendly service or help with local products, a nearby pharmacy or other specialty shop might be a better option.

title: If there was less than 1 star I would give that
body: If there was less than 1 star, I would give that. This rating goes to sephora.fr customer service for an online order within the EU. In over 500 euros order, I purchased a Sisley Paris face cream and the cream I received was from a batch that is already more than 6Y old. I wrote to Sephora immediately and they said they will run an internal investigation. It took from 26th July until 26th Aug to receive a feedback and I had to remind them constantly, every time with a response that the investigation is still ongoing. After a month and receiving a response that 6Y old cream for which I paid 300 euros is good enough to be used, which is unacceptable to me, I want to return the product however I cannot print out the return form any longer as, during their investigation, the return period has expired. Customer service is sending generic responses and the actual support is below any expectation from a respectable company as Sephora. Extremely displeased and disappointed!!! Will explore every legal route I can follow as a customer within the EU to get it right as this is outrageous.

title: No apology to 14 year old for THEIR mistake
body: My 14 year old daughter went into the Pontault Combault store in Paris while on a school trip. She was not provided with a receipt for her purchase and was then stopped by the security guard after she left the store and asked her to empty her pockets out in front of everyone, he then OPENED her purchase. She was made to return to the store where they found her receipt still in the printer - no apology and no replacement for the item the security guard opened. Why he felt he needed to break the seal on it I don't understand! Shop not interested when I emailed them on her return home.No way to treat customers!

body: Bought a product in there Val’ Europe store and then headed to the airport. When I opened it when I got home the eyeshadow palette was smashed to bits. Tried to resolve this with customer services only to be told it’s my fault as I should have checked it as the buyer before I purchased. There was no physical damage on the outside of the box and it wasn’t until I opened it I noticed the eyeshadow has been damaged. The product had been in my handbag the whole trip and was not damaged by me. I can’t open the box in store to check, so how was this my fault? Will not be purchasing from Sephora again. Total scam of a company.

title: SEPHORA THUMBS DOWN
body: When I’ve needed to make returns for an online purchase it’s been a nightmare. I live in a small town with no Sephora store, no FedEx, no UPS. I also have no home computer, no laptop, no printer, no WiFi, only a phone plan. Automated customer service is full of misinformation, confusion and communication that conflicts with the things an actual person tells you. That is, if you actually get to speak with a real person; they are usually not available. When they are, they are ignorant of basic company information and always “need to talk to my manager”. In the hope that you’ll give up and hang up they keep you on hold until you eventually get cut off. Eventually I had to drive to the closest library and figure out how to print off a shipping label and make the return via USPS. Now I’m expected to wait 30 days for a credit because returns aren’t made to Sephora but to a return center middleman. If I could give zero stars to this company I would! They need to provide the same level of quality service to everyone and realize that some of us don’t have the convenience of technology or big city living at our fingertips.

title: No Christmas gifts because of the strike
body: After putting an order on Black Friday to make sure I will have a few Sephora Christmas gift to give. I’ve checked the status of my order to discover that I should receive my package on the 6 of January so after Christmas. They did not think to send me an email advising me of this situation. When I called the customer service, they only offered to refund my purchase and offer me to get the items myself but on different stores because they did not have it all in the store close to my house. They only thing I got from the customer service representative is : « I’m sorry but it’s not our fault it’s the strike at Canada Post Office. It’s not like the strike wasn’t predictable. They should have prepared and use another shipping service.

body: Terrible costumer service! Ordered my package with a worth of €300,- in it. It would get delivered in a parcel locker. I hope whoever found it funny to take the package, I hope your very happy with it. Tried contacting them about a 100 times and got the same exact answer every single time. It’s really annoying since they are the only website who sell certain brands and they know it damn well, so they know they can do their customers dirty since most people will return anyway.Worst Costumer service I have ever experienced.

title: American in Paris- sephora sucks
body: I live between Paris and Los Angeles and I am currently in Paris. I’ve tried to order grande lash online 4 times in the last week and every one of my orders gets declined. I was charged on august 30th for a 32oz grande lash I still haven’t been refunded for. I love your product so much I even begged my husband to try and buy it on his card thinking mine was just declining, he tried four times over two days and that was also declined without explanation. I love grande lash and I am desperate and in Paris for the next month. I’ve contacted Sephora France (on Aug 30th) and they told me they would call back but I haven’t heard from them. I also sent an email (on Aug 30th) and I haven’t heard back. Since it’s been a week I’m contacting your company straight up so that you know Sephora France is not doing anything to help me understand why I can’t get this product I always buy. I have the money, the valid address, the card etc. I’m a sephora member, and they have no reasoning. Because I’m American the French people won’t give me the time of day with my poor French accent and every time I call they end up saying “someone will contact you back!” I’ve called twice. Nobody does. This whole Sephora france stuff is a scam, don’t buy it online.

title: Use of bank details without consent.
body: If I could rate 0, I would. I have succumbed to a con in that an order I made in March 2023 has been re-sent to me in April 2023. I only ordered this item once however it has been issued again ( at a much inflated price £35.63) . It appears it was sent under the auspices of Subscribe and Save. It has been taken from my bank account when I clearly did not authorise my bank details to be retained by Sephora.I am furious and cannot even get this company on the telephone. They advised me of the order via emAIL with 2 question marks which obviously represented the 2 bottles of Redkin I bought in March - they did not even state the items that were being sent. How can this be allowed.Disgusting !! I am so annoyed. I do not want these products again and I want the money back in my bank account NOW !!!Sylvia Millar

title: Order to Ireland cancelled with no reason
body: Placed a significant order during the Black Friday sale with a promise date in the following week. I could track the delivery but it was showing as not picked up yet after 1 week. I started to log queries for the next 2 weeks (in French). I finally got a response 3 weeks later with no explanation except to say that my order was cancelled and giving me a refund. Maybe Sephora don't want to deliver outside France even though they give that option. Won't be ordering again despite being a long time global Sephora customer.- 11-Jan: Follow up to Sephora reply below. It is a pity this wasn't suggested when I first enquired instead of my order being cancelled immediately - however I have no wish to follow up (especially via costly international telephone call) as I have been able to order most of the items elsewhere and won't be trying with Sephora again. If you genuinely wish to improve this, you can review my order history internally order 7025786263.

title: I was visiting sephora gare…
body: I was visiting sephora gare montparnasse after long walk to find it and the bill was 200 euros for me and my daughters, when I asked the cashier there to make me the tax refund she said cannot be done unless I have the original passport although i had a hard copy for security purpose for not walking with original passport and all shops accepted copy, and she said you should say that you want to do it before you pay then when I told her you have to follow the law of the country here and make the tax refund she asked me to leave the shop cause its time to close and waving with her finger to go out with a rude and impolite way immediately after receiving the money, I told her I will get the passport tomorrow she was talking in unacceptable way which I never saw it from any branch of sephora all over the world, when I told her will complaint she said please do, I wonder if your employees know about the customer services and satisfaction??? I asked to see her manager and she came and said to come tomorrow what if I was going to leave the following day ??! I have to go back all the way to take my right ??!!!Happened on 21st July 2022 at 7.50 pm

title: At Sephora (France) once you’re black…
body: At Sephora (France) once you’re black they put security guards on you from the moment you enter any of their stores at any location across the country. Then they never have black makeup artists so you get wrong advice. I bought a fenty powder the wrong shade because of poor advice. When I tried it out at home it was the wrong shade and because I opened the product they didn’t accept to exchange the product or refund me. And then they were proposing me to get another assistance from their white counterparts. No thank you! I wrote to their customers service on messenger, but no response.

title: Customer service fixed the issue!
body: **UPDATE**Customer service reached out to me and I got the option for a refund or a new package being send out. My second parcel is on it’s way to Belgium at this moment, hopefully all goes well with this one! Thank you, Sephora!Since they started using DPD for delivery to Belgium it has been a straight up scam. Packages are traveling up and down delivery centers to parcelstores and back but never get to me within the week, sometimes even longer, get lost, …. They straight up lie about the reasons, saying that I wasn’t home when I work from home, that the parcel store was closed etc but when I called them they were open! When you call them they never know the reasons of the delay or anything. When you contact Sephora.fr they just don’t care, yeah, if a refund of the shipping costs of €5,95 is caring, well …. Probably my stuff is damaged now because of all the times it has been in and out of delivery vehicles but wait, if I recieve something damaged I have to send it back before I can get a refund, it will cost me around €16 and I have to do it with DPD all over again?! What a service is this?! I battled through it the last couple of times, but the delivery of this package is the last straw, I won’t order here ever again as long as they use DPD. A billion dollar company but still not willing to pay a decent courier service, that’s just spitting in your paying customers face! The US would be ashamed.

title: Dear Sephora Team,
body: Dear Sephora Team,Am writing this email to report an incident happened to myself (name: Dalia Mohamed Anwar, passport number...A22208238.) and my mother ( hedayt .el ezabyThe incident happened on the 8th of June at 11.00am when we visited Sephora store (Champs Élysée branch ) and we experienced a very bad situation which was very humiliating in front of all people inside.The issue occurred when we started to pay the amount required for our purchases which was around €500, my mother's showed up her credit card and a staff member called Ms Cristal asked for an official ID, we gave her the passport as requested.Ms Crystal commented that there is a difference between the official ID and the credit card in one letter in my mother's first name (E instead of A) ( hedayt/hadayt) then we gave her the evidence that proves that both names are the same.We paid the money and when we started then to process the Tax-free, Ms Crystal was unhelpful and treated us in a very bad way, when we asked her to talk in a better way, she became more aggressive, rude, nervous and shouted on us in front of all people, we asked for the manager to report the incident but unfortunately she informed us that she is the only manager available at that time and it ended up by kicking us out of the store by force.As a result of what happened from the lady, my mother was in a very bad condition as she is a cardiac patient, and she got a hypertensive attack for what she has experienced in Sephora store.We took an immediate action by calling Sephora customer service and reported all what happened.The big surprise was when we were at the airport (going back to our country) and discovered that Ms Crystal cancelled our Tax Free.Kindly we need a reply to the followings:- Who gave the lady the authority to cancel our tax free without informing us.- Where is the document of our tax-free cancellation and who signed it.- What is Sephora's staff code of conduct policy.Please note that at Sephora store we have been:- humiliated- mistreated- insulted- intimatedAccordingly, we need to escalate the issue and to raise an official complaint and we are requesting a formal investigation as soon as possible.

title: Fast delivery and friendly customer service
body: I ordered a couple or products and they arrived in about a week. They are all fine, I only ordered makeup, so nothing that could easily break. They're all the same products I ordered, so I happy.I also had a good customer experience over the phone and via chat, when I was not able to pay initially and everyone was polite and helpful.I also liked how at the end of the order you can select 5 free products, which not many companies do online.I will order again because I love Sephora's products and I was satisfied with ordering from Sephora.fr.I wanted to leave this review, because I saw so many negative ones and didn't want to believe it's THAT bad. So here we go folks! I am a happy client! :DJ'ai commandé quelques produits et ils sont arrivés en une semaine environ. Ils sont tous très bien, je n'ai commandé que du maquillage, donc rien qui puisse se casser facilement. Ce sont tous les mêmes produits que ceux que j'avais commandés, donc je suis contente.J'ai également eu une bonne expérience client par téléphone et par chat, lorsque je n'ai pas pu payer initialement et tout le monde a été poli et serviable.J'ai également apprécié le fait qu'à la fin de la commande, vous pouvez sélectionner 5 produits gratuits, ce que peu d'entreprises font en ligne.Je commanderai à nouveau car j'aime les produits Sephora et j'ai été satisfaite de ma commande sur Sephora.fr.Je voulais laisser cet avis, parce que j'en ai vu beaucoup de négatifs et je ne voulais pas croire que c'était si mauvais. Alors voilà les amis ! Je suis une cliente heureuse ! :D

title: It looked like a very bad experience, but turned out to be very good after all!
body: I order from Sephora France last week. Everything was great till I got a mail this morning from delivery service that my package it's returning to Sephora. Problem was wrong adress - I live in Croatia, not in France. Anyway, I send them two mails, but than I have read lots of negative reviews in Trustpilot. I tried to contact them threw their Facebook page but Facebook is automatically redirecting me to Sephora in USA. I also tried to call them on phone that is listed for orders from abroad, but the automat is on French!! I don't understand anything!!!! If it is for order from abroad, it should be on English!!!Edit: Some how I got Sephora France on their Facebook page and they were very helpful. It was my fault that I did't write that I live in Croatia, so my package returned to Sephora France. They said they will send me package again. But two days after I got mail from Sephora that theey have refunded me (money was on my account two days after that). I contacted them again on Facebook page and they explained that my package have broken during shipping, so they made a refund. Also, they gave me a code so that I can buy parfume again on the same price that was on BF when I ordered it. So, if you have problems, contact them on their Facebook page.

title: Shameless scammers
body: Upon ordering I realized that the marked delivery date would be cutting it short for my trip, so I called client service to verify if I could get the order on time. In reply, a very uninterested girl told me that there is no way of knowing when the order will come. Of course, the order came a week later than expected and now I learn that the 6min call to customer service cost me 10EUR?!Will never shop at Sephora again.

title: LVMH you should be ashamed to carry this chain
body: Placed an order on French website as I live in the UK and was given a voucher as a gift.Order didn’t arrive yet I was told it was delivered... to whom I do not know, but it certainly wasn’t to me.Firstly, if Sephora has decided to cater to English speaking countries, have the decency to act accordingly and employ English speaking customer service people available to deal with queries originating from those countries. I don’t see why I should be further inconvenienced by having to translate a whole cock and bull story about where the order is.Secondly, do we live in 1920 or 2020? You want me to HANDWRITE a report about what happened? Seriously, remove your online presence, you are ill equipped to deal with it.LVMH this is a poor, poor show.I want a refund. I want someone who SPEAKS ENGLISH to assist me and I DO NOT want a rubbish e-mail address, phone number or Facebook Page reference.Employ actual people who are capable of doing their jobs. You sure charge consumers enough to be able to pay more employees.Your internal logistics issues should not be made to be the customer’s problem.Disgusting.

title: Sephora France Took My Money For Products Never Received
body: I ordered two perfume items from Sephora France on two separate orders on their website. I live in the US. The order page did not say they don’t ship to the US, and I was allowed to complete the purchase and order with my US address.But then my first order was sent to some strange French address via La Poste. I contacted La Poste by phone using Skype phone because La Poste has an English option. They emailed me a photo of how the package was sent to a wrong address and told me I would need to speak to Sephora France directly to have them address it.Problem is, you can’t reach Sephora France. There is no English option on the phone system. And I have sent at least three emails inquiring about my orders via their website and have received no response. I need the almost $200 I spent on both orders refunded - since it is obvious that I will never receive the items I paid for. I would rather have the perfumes. But it seems Sephora France really doesn’t care to either ensure I get what I paid for or to issue a refund.Anyone else have this experience? What are my options to file a formal complaint?

title: As long as the products survive the…
body: As long as the products survive the shipping to Germany, everything is fine. But in my recent order I got a broken, hence unusable highlight palette. I found the contact form... no response... filled it out again... no response. Then I went to Instagram... same result. Today I tried calling the phone number for foreigners several times. Nothing!I am quite upset- I thought Germany was a service wasteland. Turns out France is fierce competition.@Sephora.fr: If you see this review please don’t invite me to call that French number or to message your Facebook page. As a German customer Facebook redirects me to the Sephora.de FB page. As a company that ships internationally you MUST be able to serve international, non-french speaking customers!

title: I would have given 0 star if there is…
body: I would have given 0 star if there is an option. I ordered €500 worth of product online and my package got stolen. I contacted the customer service by email immediately since phone calls is never an option for a foreigner. First of all, they asked me to write a hand written consent, I did it and saved as png file because I am a Mac user. They didn’t reply to me until 3 days later, With a simple sentence, sorry, I can’t open this file, please send my another format. I was being too nice and send them another format. Yet, after 1 month, they replied back with a simple sentence again telling me the investigation is closed and the package has been delivered, no refund will be given. Ok, where is the proof???? At least they should tell me who took my package isn’t it??? You made me waited for so long and tell me nothing???? I will not purchase from Sephora France anymore, there are so many brands I can chose from.5/25 editSephora asked my to contanct them via emails, yes, sure, the phone number you gave me is never foreigner-friendly, the hotline apparently only works for people who speak french, ironically, I have been quarantined for 2 months without getting a chance to learn French. I contacted the customer service via facebook, they still asked me to wait, I asked the customer representative how long I need to wait today, they redirected me to another automatic reply page...woahhhh... I guess one month is not enough for Sephora to investigate their own answer. Is it that hard for you to get me the person who sign the pacakge, if you actually INVESTIGATED MY CASE???

title: the silent costumer service Such fun!
body: On April 5 I ordered a 160€ order to Portugal and till this day I haven’t received it, I mean my order hasn’t even shipped but... I tried to contact them through costumer service twice and they haven’t responded me back. I asked for a refund and no response either.Honestly I do not recommend you buy on the Sephora.fr website, you spend your money never receive your products and it honestly looks like I won’t have a refund either. Your just giving Sephora money without getting anything in return, just disappointement! It’s no fun and I will never buy anything there again. They are a huge company with huge amounts of money and they can’t even get a proper costumer service. Just buy from a indie company support small businesses and you actually get your makeup!They don’t even deserve the 1 star rating!Just do your self a favor and don’t buy from them!Update June 5thSephora.fr replied to my review on trustpilot but never replied to both my emails sent to their costumer service! I think it is a bit stupid that I have to contact them via facebook, why not just check the email I sent them? Well... I can say I have NEVER waited this long for a purchase. I start to get worried that I may receive my makeup expired ! On a more serious note I really don’t understand what is going on, did they forget to send me my products, did my order get lost in their warehouse? Is this just normal in this website? What the heck is going on!!!!! I never expected this from the Sephora company but I will definitely not buy from any Sephora ever again!!! And unfortunately it looks like I won’t receive my products nor a refund.Oh wait I almost forget it was given me a foreign cell number, are they expecting me to spend another pay check just to call them, from Portugal to France??? Yeah right

title: Would never step foot into another Sephora for as long as I live
body: I have just had the worst experience inside Sephora at Beaugrenelle Mall in Paris. I was with my younger sister helping her to find her shade today in Dior. We tried 3 different bottles as her skin tone is very deep and couldn’t get an exact match straight away. We sat at the tables with the chairs, mirrors and makeup remover at the centre of the store. Whilst we were there applying her makeup, one of the assistant women (seemed Moroccan) in the most aggressive tone comes to let us know that we’d have to leave the area shortly because they need the entire table (of 6 seats, 3 towards the front and 3 towards the back) for a “Guerlain Class” happening soon. I turned to ask her if she needed every seat because we are only occupying one. She said yes (aggressively again) we’d need to leave the ENTIRE table and they needed both front and back “very soon”. My sister and I purposely continued to do her makeup at the table to see when this so called class would be starting and why this woman saw two black women who would have genuinely made some purchases after trying a few shades to leave the table we used to do so. We stayed for 30 minutes more! 30 minutes for the class “happening soon” STILL no sign of such a class. Another attendant came by, saw us and carried on her merrily way. Never informing us of any class which we by then figured, was the first female attendant’s nasty way of telling us (black people) to leave the store or not be too comfortable trying on the makeup. I’ve had a similar experience each time at a Sephora when asking for samples. They are always “unavailable” or “out of stock” “sorry we can’t offer you samples”. Meanwhile I would see white women being given right next to me in the same store, samples of perfumes and makeup upon demand. Or after I purchase an item, they would then sneakily add the samples I wanted originally in with my purchased pack. This is the last straw for me. I have put up with this for too long and for my little sister to experience this blatant nasty racist woman at this store, would never make me return and give my money to this establishment. I will purchase directly from the brands themselves that cater to black women. Good bye Sephora!

title: Intimidating Security
body: The service from the ladies in store just about made my experience okay, the security, however, was incredibly intimidating and put me off coming back to Sephora in Nice. I was followed very obviously around the store by the Male security officer, even after I paid for my items and even when I was waiting outside the store waiting for my friends to finish. I moved outside because I felt so intimidated by this memeber of staff yet he followed us anyway.Please look into retraining your security.

title: Very disappointed
body: Very disappointed with the service I received at Sephora in Montpellier. I was looking for a foundation and told the lady helping me that I did not want a Dior product because I had one previously and it bothered my skin and the perfum smell gave me a headache. Somehow, she starts trying to convince me into another Dior product (I'm pretty sure these girls are receiving a commission for selling Dior because its the only product they're pushing). She tells me that it has very little perfum in it and will be good for my sensitive skin. The entire store smells like someone who is experimenting with perfum for the first time, so it was very hard to tell if the smell would bother me. After buying this product, I opened it up to see if I could handle the smell and it was unbearable. I got a headache instantly. I took it back to the store in the hopes that they would help me find the right foundation that I asked for in the first place and they refused to help me because I had already opened it. I find this absolutely absurd. The Canadian Sephora has no problem taking back a product after it has been lightly opened and I can't believe that I did not receive the same customer service here in Montpellier. I will certainly never be shopping there again.

title: Sephora is an amazing brand
body: Sephora is an amazing brand. I always fill up my suitcase when on holiday in Europe and order from Sephora.fr. I have found the service to be excellent and on the one occasion when there was an issue (one of the bubble baths split and leaked over my whole order, by e-mailing Sephora with photo's, I was offered a refund for the damaged items or a replacement. All sorted very quickly.The only reason I have not awarded 5 stars is that there has now been a limit on the number of items you can order from the UK which makes shipping expensive if the 15 items are face masks but not so if they large gift sets. It doesn't make sense as surely it is the weight that counts not number of items?Keep up the good work and please come back to the UK!

title: Beware Nuxe oils
body: I love Nuxe oils, particularly the Nuxe Oil Prodigieuse which gives a bronze glow to my winter skin! However, the applicator on my last bottle jammed the third time I used it so that no oil could come out. I contacted Nuxe customer service who asked for batch number etc which I immediately supplied, and sent to them with a photo of the bottle. Since then, and despite promising to send a replacement, nothing has happened. The first excuse was 'busy at Xmas', now they are ignoring my emails. Never again! The oil is expensive, sold as a luxury, and it was a present to myself. I'll buy a different oil next time from a company with proper customer service.

title: Amazing Customer Service
body: Dear Nuxe Customer Service,I hope this message finds you well. I am writing to express my sincere gratitude for the outstanding customer service I recently experienced with your company.A few weeks ago, I contacted your customer service team regarding an issue with a product I had received, the NUXE Huile Prodigieuse Or 100 ml. Specifically, I had encountered a recurring problem with the vaporizer component, which had broken for the second time.I want to commend your team for their prompt and efficient handling of my concern. The first instance of the vaporizer malfunction was promptly addressed, with a hassle-free product exchange facilitated by your team. I genuinely appreciated the level of professionalism and customer-centric approach demonstrated during that interaction.However, when the replacement vaporizer subsequently broke after only a few uses, I admittedly had concerns about the product's durability. I contacted your customer service team once again, outlining the situation and expressing my desire for a resolution.To my immense satisfaction, your team not only acknowledged my concerns promptly but also provided a comprehensive and satisfactory solution. It was through a new oil or a replacement vaporizer of the highest quality, your commitment to resolving the issue did not go unnoticed.I want to express my gratitude for the exemplary customer service I received throughout this process. It is evident that your team values customer satisfaction and is dedicated to ensuring a positive customer experience.In a market where exceptional service is increasingly rare, your company has truly stood out. I am now not only a satisfied customer but also an advocate for your brand, sharing my positive experience with friends and family.Thank you once again for turning what could have been a frustrating experience into a testament to your company's commitment to excellence. I look forward to continuing my association with your brand and recommending it to others.Sincerely

title: I was sent used second hand products.
body: I purchased 2 gift sets with Nuxe during the after Christmas sale. One of the sets APPEARED absolutely perfect, the other one - the tamper seal had been opened and sticky taped over, which I wasn't too happy about, but as I was treating myself, not gifting it to anyone else I would have overlooked it had it not been for the fact that two of the four products had clearly been opened, the lip balm had a gouge in it and the hand cream tamper plastic had been removed and badly placed back on. This could have easily been missed by me had I not spotted the sticky tape on the box and only found out too late (I store things until I need them) to send back or it could have been a present for someone and been a massive disappointment to them. I have sent a message to Nuxe (as they avoid putting a telephone number on their website, although they are happy to ask for yours) to try and get a NEW replacement and postage costs/label to send it back, as I don't see why I should be out of pocket for their error. I cannot believe a supposedly high end manufacturer would make this kind of mistake, surely they should have some sort of quality control checking returns haven't been opened and used, so the don't end up flogging used germ filled goods to unsuspecting customers who are making fresh purchases in good faith.

title: Bad business ethics and poor Customer Service
body: After trying a Nuxe product I was happy with I decided to buy from this site since they were offering appealing discounts,But none of the three products I bought was satisfactory and the expiration date on TWO of them was a lot shorter than customary in cosmetics. I only checked it after the night cream I bought (Nuxuriance) smelled rancid.I wrote to them immediately (they do not have a customer service telephone Nº) and for the last week have been dealing with someone who either does not understand English too well or does not read your messages.After providing the batch number (twice) and exchanging several messages I got this reply, TODAY: "After asking to our Quality department with the batch number, they have made controls and informed us that is the normal evolution of this product ".What they mean by "normal evolution" is anybody's guess.I am still waiting them to confirm what exactly they are going to refund if I send the unopened third product, i.e., if they are going to reimburse the cost of sending it (they did not provide a return label) and if they are also going to charge me for the 4 pounds initial delivery which I got free because I spent a certain amount, which would not be the case if they substract the serum I want to return.Appalling customer service and poor practices. Avoid!!

title: Pas dans creneau promis, beaucoup de mauvais informations, deplacer pour rien, etc.
body: I was promised delivery between Jan 8-Jan 11. When I heard nothing as of Jan 11, I sent a message and was told the package was waiting at the Relais Colis. On Jan 12, I received an email saying the package was sent and would arrive between Jan 8-Jan 11. On Jan 13, I went to the relais colis and was told they no longer do Relais Colis! Later on Jan 13, I received an email saying the Relais Colis that I chose no longer is part of the network and I had 24 hours to choose another or they would choose one themselves. The closest location I could find is over 3 km away and will be many, many days later than the latest promised.

title: Products are great, customer service in HORRIBLE
body: I have purchased almost 200€ worth of Nuxe products over the last 6 weeks. The products are very nice. Unfortunately they have a SERIOUS PROBLEM with their customer service. I discovered their 'carte fidelity' program online after my second purchase. Those items packaged in boxes have secret codes inside the boxes. These work and can be registered no problem. But the Huile Prodigieuse doesn't come in a box so you have to submit copies of your "factures", your reciepts to be credited. That's fair enough. But what is so INCREDIBILY STUPID is they don't accept thier OWN ONLINE FACTURES as proof of purchase. I have been back and forth 3 times with their customer (non) service people arguing about this. I'm so fed up I think I will never buy another Nuxe product again. What's the point of having a carte fidelity if it's just a horrible experience for your clients....just dumb, really stupid.
